- docs-updater: Docs Updater Agent
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<project xmlns="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0"
         xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
         xsi:schemaLocation="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0 http://maven.apache.org/xsd/maven-4.0.0.xsd">
    <modelVersion>4.0.0</modelVersion>

    <parent>
        <groupId>fan.summer</groupId>
        <artifactId>SwissKit-parent</artifactId>
        <version>3.0.0-SNAPSHOT</version>
        <relativePath>../pom.xml</relativePath>
    </parent>

    <artifactId>SwissKit</artifactId>
    <version>3.0.0</version>

    <dependencies>

        <!-- SwissKitJ-Api: bundled in fat-JAR at runtime so plugins can load against it -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>fan.summer.api</groupId>
            <artifactId>SwissKitJ-Api</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <!-- JavaFX: the main app is the runtime provider, so compile scope to bundle in fat-JAR -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.openjfx</groupId>
            <artifactId>javafx-graphics</artifactId>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.openjfx</groupId>
            <artifactId>javafx-controls</artifactId>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.openjfx</groupId>
            <artifactId>javafx-web</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <!-- Lombok -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.projectlombok</groupId>
            <artifactId>lombok</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <!-- Excel -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.apache.fesod</groupId>
            <artifactId>fesod-sheet</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <!-- H2 Database -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.h2database</groupId>
            <artifactId>h2</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <!-- Mybatis -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.mybatis</groupId>
            <artifactId>mybatis</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <!-- Email -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.simplejavamail</groupId>
            <artifactId>simple-java-mail</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <!-- MigLayout (for legacy Swing components pending JavaFX migration) -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>com.miglayout</groupId>
            <artifactId>miglayout-swing</artifactId>
            <version>11.3</version>
        </dependency>

        <!-- Force override vulnerable version transitively brought in by simple-java-mail -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.eclipse.angus</groupId>
            <artifactId>angus-mail</artifactId>
        </dependency>

        <!-- Logging: slf4j API + logback binding -->
        <dependency>
            <groupId>org.slf4j</groupId>
            <artifactId>slf4j-api</artifactId>
        </dependency>
        <dependency>
            <groupId>ch.qos.logback</groupId>
            <artifactId>logback-classic</artifactId>
        </dependency>
    </dependencies>

    <profiles>
        <profile>
            <id>windows-exe</id>
            <activation>
                <os>
                    <family>windows</family>
                </os>
            </activation>
            <build>
                <plugins>
                    <plugin>
                        <groupId>com.akathist.maven.plugins.launch4j</groupId>
                        <artifactId>launch4j-maven-plugin</artifactId>
                        <version>2.7.0</version>
                        <executions>
                            <execution>
                                <id>l4j-clui</id>
                                <phase>package</phase>
                                <goals>
                                    <goal>launch4j</goal>
                                </goals>
                                <configuration>
                                    <headerType>gui</headerType>
                                    <outfile>target/SwissKit.exe</outfile>
                                    <jar>target/SwissKitJ-${project.version}.jar</jar>
                                    <icon>src/main/resources/icons/SwissKit.ico</icon>
                                    <errTitle>SwissKit Error</errTitle>
                                    <downloadUrl>https://adoptium.net/</downloadUrl>
                                    <supportUrl>https://github.com/MuskStark/SwissKitJ</supportUrl>
                                    <jre>
                                        <minVersion>17</minVersion>
                                        <jdkPreference>preferJre</jdkPreference>
                                        <runtimeBits>64/32</runtimeBits>
                                    </jre>
                                </configuration>
                            </execution>
                        </executions>
                    </plugin>
                </plugins>
            </build>
        </profile>
    </profiles>

    <build>
        <resources>
            <resource>
                <directory>src/main/resources</directory>
                <filtering>false</filtering>
            </resource>
        </resources>
        <plugins>
            <!-- Maven Shade Plugin -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-shade-plugin</artifactId>
                <version>3.5.3</version>
                <executions>
                    <execution>
                        <phase>package</phase>
                        <goals>
                            <goal>shade</goal>
                        </goals>
                        <configuration>
                            <!-- 排除 JavaFX 模块签名，避免模块系统冲突 -->
                            <filters>
                                <filter>
                                    <artifact>org.openjfx:*</artifact>
                                    <excludes>
                                        <exclude>module-info.class</exclude>
                                    </excludes>
                                </filter>
                                <!-- 排除签名文件，防止 shade 后签名校验失败 -->
                                <filter>
                                    <artifact>*:*</artifact>
                                    <excludes>
                                        <exclude>META-INF/*.SF</exclude>
                                        <exclude>META-INF/*.DSA</exclude>
                                        <exclude>META-INF/*.RSA</exclude>
                                    </excludes>
                                </filter>
                            </filters>
                            <transformers>
                                <transformer
                                        implementation="org.apache.maven.plugins.shade.resource.ManifestResourceTransformer">
                                    <mainClass>fan.summer.Launcher</mainClass>
                                    <manifestEntries>
                                        <Implementation-Version>${project.version}</Implementation-Version>
                                    </manifestEntries>
                                </transformer>
                                <transformer
                                        implementation="org.apache.maven.plugins.shade.resource.ServicesResourceTransformer"/>
                            </transformers>
                            <finalName>SwissKitJ-${project.version}</finalName>
                            <createDependencyReducedPom>false</createDependencyReducedPom>
                        </configuration>
                    </execution>
                </executions>
            </plugin>

            <!-- Maven Compiler Plugin -->
            <plugin>
                <groupId>org.apache.maven.plugins</groupId>
                <artifactId>maven-compiler-plugin</artifactId>
                <version>3.13.0</version>
                <configuration>
                    <source>21</source>
                    <target>21</target>
                    <encoding>UTF-8</encoding>
                    <annotationProcessorPaths>
                        <path>
                            <groupId>org.projectlombok</groupId>
                            <artifactId>lombok</artifactId>
                            <version>${lombok.version}</version>
                        </path>
                    </annotationProcessorPaths>
                    <!-- Exclude ALL legacy Swing files pending JavaFX migration -->
                    <excludes>
                        <exclude>**/StartLoadingPage.java</exclude>
                        <exclude>**/HomePage.java</exclude>
                        <exclude>**/SideMenuBar.java</exclude>
                        <exclude>**/TableUtil.java</exclude>
                        <exclude>**/UIUtils.java</exclude>
                        <exclude>**/WelcomePage.java</exclude>
                        <exclude>**/EmailKitPage.java</exclude>
                        <exclude>**/EmailTagsView.java</exclude>
                        <exclude>**/EmailAddressBookView.java</exclude>
                        <exclude>**/AddAddressView.java</exclude>
                        <exclude>**/SettingKitPage.java</exclude>
                        <exclude>**/ExcelKitPage.java</exclude>
                        <exclude>**/ConfigView.java</exclude>
                        <exclude>**/ConfigEditorView.java</exclude>
                        <exclude>**/MassSentConfigView.java</exclude>
                        <exclude>**/ViewEmailSentLogView.java</exclude>
                        <exclude>**/QueryAllEmailInfoWorker.java</exclude>
                        <exclude>**/ShowConfigViewWorker.java</exclude>
                        <exclude>**/SetComplexSplitConfigWorker.java</exclude>
                        <exclude>**/ExcelSplitWorker.java</exclude>
                        <exclude>**/ExcelAnalysisWorker.java</exclude>
                        <exclude>**/ClearComplexSplitConfigWorker.java</exclude>
                        <exclude>**/EmailSentWorker.java</exclude>
                        <exclude>**/plugin/swisskitj/QccToExcel.java</exclude>
                        <exclude>**/plugin/swisskit/hpl/ui/HappyLearning.java</exclude>
                        <exclude>**/plugin/swisskit/mouse/ui/KeepMove.java</exclude>
                    </excludes>
                </configuration>
            </plugin>
        </plugins>
    </build>

</project>

<project xmlns="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0"

         xsi:schemaLocation="http://maven.apache.org/POM/4.0.0 http://maven.apache.org/xsd/maven-4.0.0.xsd">
    <modelVersion>4.0.0</modelVersion>

    <groupId>fan.summer</groupId>
    <artifactId>SwissKit-parent</artifactId>
    <version>3.0.0-SNAPSHOT</version>
    <packaging>pom</packaging>

    <properties>
        <maven.compiler.source>21</maven.compiler.source>
        <maven.compiler.target>21</maven.compiler.target>
        <project.build.sourceEncoding>UTF-8</project.build.sourceEncoding>
        <javafx.version>21.0.2</javafx.version>
        <lombok.version>1.18.42</lombok.version>
        <swisskit.api.version>3.0.0</swisskit.api.version>

        <logback.version>1.5.6</logback.version>
        <junit.jupiter.version>5.10.2</junit.jupiter.version>
    </properties>

    <dependencyManagement>
        <dependencies>
            <!-- SwissKitJ API (consumed by main app and all plugins) -->
            <dependency>
                <groupId>fan.summer.api</groupId>
                <artifactId>SwissKitJ-Api</artifactId>
                <version>${swisskit.api.version}</version>
            </dependency>

            <!-- JavaFX (provided by host app at runtime) -->
            <dependency>
                <groupId>org.openjfx</groupId>

                <version>${javafx.version}</version>
            </dependency>
            <dependency>

            </dependency>
            <dependency>

            </dependency>

            <dependency>

            </dependency>

            </dependency>
            <dependency>
                <groupId>org.slf4j</groupId>

            </dependency>
            <dependency>
                <groupId>ch.qos.logback</groupId>
                <artifactId>logback-classic</artifactId>
                <version>${logback.version}</version>
            </dependency>
            <dependency>
                <groupId>org.junit.jupiter</groupId>
                <artifactId>junit-jupiter</artifactId>
                <version>${junit.jupiter.version}</version>

        </dependencies>

    <!-- Build order: SwissKitJ-Api must be built before SwissKit, which must be built before plugins -->

    </modules>

</project>

content.thinking:
The key issue here is clear: JavaFX 21.0.2 doesn't include native code in its JARs — the platform-specific native libraries (glass, prism, etc.) need to be present on the target system. When you shade the JavaFX JARs into a fat JAR, you only get the Java class files, not the native binaries for the target platform.

Let me read the Launcher class and check if there are any JavaFX native classifier dependencies.

Now I understand the issue. Let me also check the JavaFX native dependencies. The problem is:

1. JavaFX modules (javafx-graphics, javafx-controls, javafx-web) are just Java bytecode JARs - they don't contain the native libraries (`.dll` on Windows, `.so` on Linux, `.dylib` on macOS)
2. The native libraries for each platform are in separate classifier JARs (e.g., `javafx-graphics-21.0.2-win.jar`, `javafx-graphics-21.0.2-mac.jar`, `javafx-graphics-21.0.2-linux.jar`)
3. When you build a fat JAR with maven-shade-plugin, only the platform-neutral JARs get included - NOT the platform-specific native ones

This is exactly why the app can't run on other machines. The fat JAR built on the developer's Mac includes macOS native libraries (if any were included via classifiers), but when you take that same JAR to a Windows or Linux machine, the native graphics/glass libraries for those platforms are missing.

Let me check if there are any classifier-specific JavaFX dependencies being pulled in.

content.thinking:
Now I can see the problem clearly. The fat JAR contains:
- `libglass.dylib` - macOS native library
- `libjavafx_font.dylib` - macOS native library
- `libprism_es2.dylib` - macOS native library
- `libprism_sw.dylib` - macOS native library
- `libprism_common.dylib` - macOS native library
- `libdecora_sse.dylib` - macOS native library
- `libjavafx_iio.dylib` - macOS native library
- `javafx-swt.jar` - this shouldn't be here!

All these `.dylib` files are macOS-only. This fat JAR was built on macOS, so it grabbed the macOS native libraries. When you run this on Windows, it needs `.dll` files, and on Linux it needs `.so` files. These are simply not present.

Also, there's `javafx-swt.jar` which is the SWT interop JAR and shouldn't be in the fat JAR at all.

The root cause is confirmed: **The fat JAR only contains the macOS native libraries from the developer's machine where the JAR was built.** To fix this, there are a few approaches:

1. **Use the `javafx-maven-plugin`** to create a proper runtime image with `jlink` (best practice for JavaFX apps)
2. **Add all platform classifier dependencies** to the shade plugin so the fat JAR contains native libs for all platforms
3. **Use the JavaFX platform-specific classifier JARs** explicitly

The best approach for a desktop app like SwissKit would be to use `jlink` to create a custom runtime that includes the JavaFX modules. But since the project already uses Launch4j for Windows EXE generation, a more practical fix would be:

For the fat JAR approach - include all platform classifier dependencies. Let me also check if the user wants cross-platform support or just specific platforms.

Actually, looking more carefully, the simplest and most robust solution is to include the platform-specific JavaFX classifier JARs for all three platforms (win, mac, linux) in the fat JAR. This way, regardless of which platform the JAR is built on, the native libraries for all platforms are bundled.

But wait - including all platforms' native libs would make the JAR very large. Let me think about the best approach.

Actually, the standard approach for JavaFX desktop apps is to use `javafx-maven-plugin` + `jlink` to create platform-specific distributions. Let me suggest this approach.

But the user might just want a quick fix. The simplest fix is:
1. Add explicit dependencies on the JavaFX classifier JARs for all three platforms in the SwissKit pom.xml
2. These classifier JARs contain the native `.dll`/`.so`/`.dylib` files

Let me advise the user on the options and suggest the best path forward.

content.text:
问题定位到了。你的 fat JAR 里只包含了 **macOS 的原生库**（`.dylib`），没有 Windows 的 `.dll` 和 Linux 的 `.so`。这是构建机器的平台决定的——你在 Mac 上打包，Maven 只拉取了 macOS 的 JavaFX 原生库。

```
jar tf SwissKitJ-3.0.0.jar | grep -E '\.(dylib|dll|so)$'
libjavafx_iio.dylib      ← macOS only
libglass.dylib           ← macOS only  
libjavafx_font.dylib     ← macOS only
libprism_common.dylib    ← macOS only
libprism_es2.dylib       ← macOS only
libprism_sw.dylib        ← macOS only
libdecora_sse.dylib      ← macOS only
# 没有 .dll 和 .so 文件
```

JavaFX 的 JAR 分两层：
- **平台无关 JAR**：只有 Java 字节码（`javafx-graphics-21.0.2.jar`）
- **平台原生 JAR**：包含 `.dll`/`.so`/`.dylib`（`javafx-graphics-21.0.2-win.jar`）

修复方案是在 `SwissKit/pom.xml` 中显式添加三个平台的原生库依赖：

content.thinking:
Interesting! The classifier format is `mac-aarch64`, not `mac`. And there are no `win` or `linux` classifier JARs in the local Maven cache — they've never been downloaded because the project is built on macOS with aarch64 architecture.

The correct classifiers are:
- `mac-aarch64` (Apple Silicon Mac)

content.text:
修复完成。总结一下：

**问题根因**：JavaFX 的平台原生库（`.dll`/`.so`/`.dylib`）通过 Maven classifier 机制区分平台。打包时只自动拉取了构建机器（macOS ARM64）的原生库，所以 fat JAR 里只有 `.dylib` 文件，拿到 Windows/Linux 上缺少 `glass.dll`、`prism_es2.so` 等原生库，JavaFX 无法初始化图形子系统。

**修复方式**：在 `SwissKit/pom.xml` 中显式添加了 Windows、Linux 平台的 JavaFX classifier 依赖，maven-shade-plugin 会自动将它们合并进 fat JAR。

**修复后验证**：
```
修复前：只有 .dylib (macOS)
修复后：.dylib (macOS) + .dll (Windows) + .so (Linux) 全部齐全
JAR 大小：140MB（三平台原生库合并后的正常大小）
```

现在把 JAR 拿到其他机器上，确认目标机器安装了 JDK 21+，用 `java -jar SwissKitJ-3.0.0.jar` 就能启动了。
目标linux有应用审计系统。没有签名的程序不能启动，但是java和jar包可以运行，这对主程序有影响吗

content.thinking:
The user is asking about a Linux environment with an application auditing/whitelisting system. They say that unsigned programs can't run, but Java and JAR files can run. They want to know if this will affect the main program (SwissKit).

Let me think about this:

1. The main program is a fat JAR (`SwissKitJ-3.0.0.jar`) run via `java -jar`
2. Java and JAR files are allowed by the audit system
3. The fat JAR now contains native `.so` files (from JavaFX) which get extracted at runtime

The potential issue is: JavaFX's native libraries are bundled inside the JAR as `.so` files. When JavaFX starts up, it needs to load these native libraries. By default, JavaFX extracts native libraries from the JAR to a temporary directory (like `/tmp/javafx-*` or `~/.openjfx/cache/`) and then calls `System.load()` / `System.loadLibrary()` on them.

The question is whether the Linux audit system will block:
1. Loading `.so` files from temporary directories
2. Executing code from non-whitelisted locations

This could be a real problem because:
- The audit system might block `dlopen()` on `.so` files extracted to `/tmp` or other temporary locations
- Some security solutions (like SELinux, AppArmor, or third-party EDR/audit tools) can restrict loading of shared libraries from non-standard paths

However, since the user says "Java and JAR files can run", and Java itself loads `.so` files via JNI (which is how JavaFX native code works), the question is whether:
- Java's own native libraries (like `libjava.so`, `libjvm.so`) load fine (likely yes since Java itself is allowed)
- JavaFX's extracted native libraries would be treated differently

This depends on the specific audit system, but generally:
- If it works at the kernel level (e.g., signature verification of ELF binaries for `execve()`), then `.so` files loaded via `dlopen()` might NOT be checked, because `dlopen()` doesn't execute a new process
- If it works at the file level (e.g., checking all opened files), then extracted `.so` files might be blocked
- Java's own behavior of loading JNI libraries from the JRE's `lib/` directory is normally allowed because those paths are part of the trusted Java installation

The safest approach is to use `jlink` to create a custom runtime image where JavaFX native libraries are pre-extracted into the runtime's `lib/` directory, rather than being extracted at runtime from the JAR. But that's a bigger change.

Actually, let me also think about this from a different angle. The user's concern is specifically about the audit system blocking unsigned programs. If the issue is signature-based (like code signing certificates), then:

1. The fat JAR itself is not a signed executable - it's just a ZIP file run by `java`
2. The `.so` files inside the JAR are also not independently signed
3. When JavaFX extracts them and loads them, it's still Java's process doing the `dlopen()`

Most Linux audit/whitelist systems that say "Java is allowed" typically allow Java's entire process tree, including any JNI libraries it loads, because they're all within the same process. The "unsigned program" check usually applies to `execve()` syscalls (launching new processes), not to `dlopen()` (loading shared libraries within an already-approved process).

So the short answer is: **No, this should not be a problem.** The native `.so` libraries are loaded by the same Java process that's already whitelisted, not launched as separate programs. `dlopen()` is different from `execve()`.

However, there IS a nuance: some security systems (like SELinux with strict policies, or certain EDR products) can restrict where shared libraries can be loaded from. In that case, you'd want to either:
- Use `jlink` to put the native libs in a standard location
- Set `java.library.path` to a trusted directory
- Extract the `.so` files to a whitelisted directory before running

Let me give the user a clear, concise answer.

content.text:
**基本没有影响。** 原因在于 Linux 审计系统对"程序签名"的检查对象是**可执行文件**（`execve()` 系统调用），而 JavaFX 的 `.so` 原生库是通过 **`dlopen()`** 在 Java 进程内部加载的，不是独立启动的新进程。

具体来说：

- 审计系统看到的是 `java -jar SwissKitJ-3.0.0.jar` 这一个进程——Java 本身已经通过审计
- `.so` 文件是被 `System.loadLibrary()` / `dlopen()` 加载到同一个 Java 进程地址空间的，不会触发新的 `execve()`
- 这和 Java 自己加载 `libjvm.so`、`libjava.so` 的原理完全一样——既然 Java 能正常运行，说明这种 JNI 加载方式不会被拦截

**但是**，有一种边缘情况需要注意：如果你的审计系统有**文件完整性校验**（不仅检查进程启动，还检查 `dlopen()` 的目标文件），那 JavaFX 从 JAR 临时目录（默认 `~/.openjfx/cache/` 或 `/tmp/`）加载 `.so` 可能会被拦截。症状是启动时报 `UnsatisfiedLinkError`。

如果遇到这种情况，解决方法是在启动前手动提取 `.so` 到 JAVA 的 `lib/` 目录下，或者用 `-Djava.library.path` 指定一个受信任的路径。但按你的描述（"Java 和 jar 包可以运行"），大概率不需要，直接用 `java -jar` 即可。

lastPrompt: 目标linux有应用审计系统。没有签名的程序不能启动，但是java和jar包可以运行，这对主程序有影响吗

🐛 fix: bundle cross-platform JavaFX native libraries in fat JAR

The fat JAR only contained macOS .dylib files because Maven auto-resolved
only the build host's platform classifier. Added explicit win/mac/linux
classifier dependencies for javafx-graphics, javafx-controls, and javafx-web
so the fat JAR can run on all three platforms regardless of build host.
EOF
)"

content.content:
1	# SwissKit
2	
3	![SwissKit](https://img.shields.io/badge/SwissKit-Desktop%20Toolbox-blue) ![Java](https://img.shields.io/badge/Java-17-orange) ![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-green) ![Maven](https://img.shields.io/badge/Maven-3.6+-red)
4	
5	**SwissKit** is a *modular desktop toolbox* built with JavaFX. It provides a clean, extensible platform for various
6	utility tools including Excel file processing, email sending, and plugin support. The application uses a
7	plugin-based architecture with automatic service discovery, making it easy to add new functionality.
8	
9	If you want a lightweight, fast, and customizable desktop utility suite with a modern glassmorphism UI, this is it.
10	
11	---
12	
13	## Quick Start
14	
15	**Requirements:**
16	
17	- **JDK 17 or higher** (recommended: [Eclipse Temurin](https://adoptium.net/))
18	- **Maven 3.6 or higher**
19	
20	### Installation
21	
22	```bash
23	# Clone the repository
24	git clone https://github.com/MuskStark/SwissKitJ.git
25	cd SwissKitJ
26	
27	# Install API module first (required)
28	mvn install -f SwissKitJ-Api/pom.xml -DskipTests
29

file.content:
# SwissKit

![SwissKit](https://img.shields.io/badge/SwissKit-Desktop%20Toolbox-blue) ![Java](https://img.shields.io/badge/Java-17-orange) ![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-green) ![Maven](https://img.shields.io/badge/Maven-3.6+-red)

**SwissKit** is a *modular desktop toolbox* built with JavaFX. It provides a clean, extensible platform for various
utility tools including Excel file processing, email sending, and plugin support. The application uses a
plugin-based architecture with automatic service discovery, making it easy to add new functionality.

If you want a lightweight, fast, and customizable desktop utility suite with a modern glassmorphism UI, this is it.

---

## Quick Start

**Requirements:**

- **JDK 17 or higher** (recommended: [Eclipse Temurin](https://adoptium.net/))
- **Maven 3.6 or higher**

### Installation

```bash
# Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/MuskStark/SwissKitJ.git
cd SwissKitJ

# Install API module first (required)
mvn install -f SwissKitJ-Api/pom.xml -DskipTests

# Build the main project

file.content:
# Changelog

All notable changes to SwissKit will be documented in this file.

The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.0.0/),
and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).

---

## [Unreleased]

### 🐛 Bug Fixes

- **Plugin Loading**: Handle plugin loading errors gracefully to ensure app startup when corrupted plugins exist

---

## 1.1.0

**Release Date:** 2026-03-30

---

### ✨ New Features

- **Plugin Hot-Deployment**: Deploy, reload, and uninstall plugins without restarting the application
- **HappyLearning Enhancement**: Add class hours tracking and status display

### 🔄 Changes

- **Project Restructuring**: Renamed `Happy-learning` → `SwissKitJ-Plugin-HappyLearning`, moved plugins to `OfficalPlugin/` directory
- **Module Organization**: Added `SwissKitJ-Api` as a shared API module

---

## 1.0.0

**Release Date:** 2026-03-29

---

### Introduction

SwissKit is a modular desktop productivity toolkit built with Java Swing, designed to simplify daily office tasks. It provides powerful Excel processing capabilities, email management with mass-sending features, and a flexible plugin system for extensibility.

**Key Characteristics:**
- Cross-platform: Windows, Linux, macOS (Apple Silicon)
- Plugin Architecture: Auto-discovers and loads plugins via Java SPI
- Modern UI: FlatLaf Look and Feel
- Database: H2 embedded database with MyBatis ORM
- Requirements: Java 11 or higher

---

### Features

#### Excel Kit
- **Complex Split Mode**: Split Excel files with complex configurations
- **Configuration Editor**: Visual editor for split parameters
- **Progress Tracking**: Real-time progress bar with animation

content.thinking:
I see a potential issue. The compiler excludes use `**/Pattern` which will exclude files matching that pattern anywhere in the source tree. But the backup files are in `backup/SwissKit/java/...` which is outside the source tree (`src/main/java`). Let me check if backup files are part of any Maven compilation at all.

Actually, looking at the pom.xml, the backup directory is at `backup/SwissKit/java/` relative to the project root. The Maven compiler only compiles files in `src/main/java` by default. So the backup files are already excluded from compilation. The `excludes` in the compiler plugin are for legacy Swing files still in `src/main/java` that haven't been migrated.

Let me check if there are Swing files in `src/main/java` that need to be excluded.

Also, I notice something: the compiler plugin excludes reference **plugin Java files that are in the backup directory** (like `QccToExcel.java`, `HappyLearning.java`, `KeepMove.java`). These files should NOT be in `src/main/java` anymore — they should only be in `backup/`. The exclusions might be stale if the files have already been moved.

Let me check:

2. Are there backup files in the backup directory that correspond to the excludes?

content.content:
1	# Changelog
2	
3	All notable changes to SwissKit will be documented in this file.
4	
5	The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.0.0/),
6	and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).
7	
8	---
9	
10	## [Unreleased]
11	
12	### 🐛 Bug Fixes
13	
14	- **Plugin Loading**: Handle plugin loading errors gracefully to ensure app startup when corrupted plugins exist
15	
16	---
17	
18	## 1.1.0
19	
20	**Release Date:** 2026-03-30
21	
22	---
23	
24	### ✨ New Features
25	
26	- **Plugin Hot-Deployment**: Deploy, reload, and uninstall plugins without restarting the application
27	- **HappyLearning Enhancement**: Add class hours tracking and status display
28	
29	### 🔄 Changes
30	
31	- **Project Restructuring**: Renamed `Happy-learning` → `SwissKitJ-Plugin-HappyLearning`, moved plugins to `OfficalPlugin/` directory
32	- **Module Organization**: Added `SwissKitJ-Api` as a shared API module
33	
34	---
35	
36	## 1.0.0
37	
38	**Release Date:** 2026-03-29
39	
40	---
41	
42	### Introduction
43	
44	SwissKit is a modular desktop productivity toolkit built with Java Swing, designed to simplify daily office tasks. It provides powerful Excel processing capabilities, email management with mass-sending features, and a flexible plugin system for extensibility.
45	
46	**Key Characteristics:**
47	- Cross-platform: Windows, Linux, macOS (Apple Silicon)
48	- Plugin Architecture: Auto-discovers and loads plugins via Java SPI
49	- Modern UI: FlatLaf Look and Feel
50	- Database: H2 embedded database with MyBatis ORM
51	- Requirements: Java 11 or higher
52	
53	---
54	
55	### Features
56	
57	#### Excel Kit
58	- **Complex Split Mode**: Split Excel files with complex configurations
59	- **Configuration Editor**: Visual editor for split parameters
60	- **Progress Tracking**: Real-time progress bar with animation
61	
62	#### Email Kit
63	- **Mass Email Sending**: Send emails to recipients based on tags
64	- **Address Book**: Manage contacts with tag associations
65	- **Tag Management**: Organize recipients by tags
66	- **Sent Log**: Track email sending history with status
67	
68	#### Plugin System
69	- **Auto-Discovery**: Automatically finds plugins in `META-INF/services`
70	- **Isolated Loading**: Plugins loaded in isolated ClassLoader
71	- **Easy Extension**: Implement `KitPage` interface to add new tools
72	
73	#### Settings
74	- **Unified Configuration**: Centralized settings management
75	- **Plugin Management**: View and manage installed plugins
76	
77	---
78	
79	### Installation
80	
81	#### System Requirements
82	- Java 11 or higher (JDK/JRE)
83	- Windows 10+, Linux (glibc 2.17+), or macOS 11+
84	
85	#### Windows Installation
86	1. Download `SwissKit-windows.zip`
87	2. Extract to desired location
88	3. Run `SwissKit.exe`
89	
90	#### Linux Installation
91	1. Download `SwissKit-linux.zip`
92	2. Extract: `unzip SwissKit-linux.zip`
93	3. Run: `./run.sh`
94	
95	#### macOS Installation
96	1. Download `SwissKit-macos-apple-silicon.zip`
97	2. Extract: `unzip SwissKit-macos-apple-silicon.zip`
98	3. Run: `open SwissKit.app` or `./run.sh`
99	
100	**Note:** Java 11+ is required. Download from [Adoptium](https://adoptium.net/).
101	
102	---
103	
104	### For Developers
105	
106	#### Build from Source
107	
108	```bash
109	# Install API module to local Maven repo (required)
110	mvn install -f SwissKitJ-Api/pom.xml -DskipTests
111	
112	# Compile
113	mvn clean compile
114	
115	# Package executable JAR
116	mvn clean package -DskipTests
117	
118	# Run (development)
119	mvn exec:java -Dexec.mainClass="fan.summer.Main"
120	```
121	
122	#### Project Structure
123	```
124	SwissKitJ/
125	├── SwissKitJ-Api/                         # Shared API module (interfaces, annotations)
126	├── SwissKit/                              # Main application
127	├── OfficalPlugin/
128	│   ├── SwissKitJ-Plugin-HappyLearning/   # Auto-learning plugin
129	│   └── SwissKitJ-Plugin-Qcc/              # CSV to Excel conversion plugin
130	└── docs/                                  # Documentation
131	```
132	
133	#### Plugin Development
134	1. Create class implementing `KitPage` interface
135	2. Annotate with `@SwissKitPage`
136	3. Register in `META-INF/services/fan.summer.api.KitPage`
137	4. Tool appears automatically in sidebar
138	
139	#### API Dependency
140	```xml
141	<dependency>
142	    <groupId>fan.summer.api</groupId>
143	    <artifactId>SwissKitJ-Api</artifactId>
144	    <version>1.0.0</version>
145	</dependency>
146	```
147	
148	---
149	
150	### Changelog
151	
152	The following is the complete version history.
153	
154	---
155	
156	## 1.0.0-RC.1
157	
158	### 🔧 Fixes
159	
160	- ExcelSplitWorker: Fix EDT violation by using GradientProgressBar type instead of JProgressBar for proper animation
161	- EmailSentWorker: Division by zero guard when totalEmails is zero
162	- EmailSentWorker: NPE guard when tagCollect returns null
163	- SetComplexSplitConfigWorker: EDT violation fix - read Swing component values in constructor
164	- MassSentConfigView: NPE guard for config.getToTag()
165	- EmailKitPage: NPE guard for config.getToTag()/getCcTag() in equals comparison
166	- QueryAllEmailInfoWorker: NPE guard when JSON.parseArray returns null
167	- ConfigEditorView: Add null-safe objToString() helper for table values
168	- PluginLoader: Check mkdirs() return value
169	- GradientProgressBar: Stop animation timer in removeNotify() to prevent resource leaks
170	- ShowConfigViewWorker: Re-throw exceptions instead of silent catch
171	
172	### 🔄 Changes
173	
174	- Maven: Disable resource filtering to prevent binary icon files from being corrupted
175	
176	---
177	
178	## 1.0.0-Beta.4
179	
180	### 🚀 Enhancements
181	
182	- Settings: Add plugin management UI with installed plugins table and uninstall functionality
183	- HappyLearning: Display current lesson ID and name (brief) during learning sessions
184	- HappyLearning: Add progress bar text display showing "current/max h" format
185	
186	### 🔧 Fixes
187	
188	- EDT violations: SetComplexSplitConfigWorker and ClearComplexSplitConfigWorker now properly wrap UI updates in SwingUtilities.invokeLater()
189	- Resource leaks: EmailSentWorker SqlSession usage restructured with try-with-resources
190	- Resource leaks: ExcelUtil Workbook leaks fixed on exception paths
191	- NullPointerExceptions: Fixed in EmailSentWorker (progressBar, emailTags), EmailKitPage (tagList), EmailTagsView (parseLong), Main.java (getCause())
192	- Silent failures: QueryAllEmailInfoWorker now returns null instead of empty list on error for proper error handling
193	- Cancellation support: EmailSentWorker mass-sending loop now checks isCancelled()
194	
195	---
196	
197	## 1.0.0-Beta.3
198	
199	### 🚀 Enhancements
200	
201	- Email Address Book: Add double-click editing functionality for contacts
202	- Email Address Book: Reset button now clears only tag information
203	
204	---
205	
206	## 1.0.0-Beta.2
207	
208	### 🔧 Fixes
209	
210	- PluginLoader: IsolatedPluginClassLoader now properly delegates JDK classes (java.*, javax.*, sun.*, com.sun.*) to main
211	  app ClassLoader
212	- PluginLoader: Universal classloading fallback ensures plugin DTOs and third-party library classes are accessible from
213	  isolated plugin JARs
214	
215	### 📝 Documentation
216

# Changelog

All notable changes to SwissKit will be documented in this file.

The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.0.0/),
and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).

---

## [Unreleased]

### 🐛 Bug Fixes

- **Plugin Loading**: Handle plugin loading errors gracefully to ensure app startup when corrupted plugins exist

---

## 1.1.0

**Release Date:** 2026-03-30

---

### ✨ New Features

- **Plugin Hot-Deployment**: Deploy, reload, and uninstall plugins without restarting the application
- **HappyLearning Enhancement**: Add class hours tracking and status display

### 🔄 Changes

- **Project Restructuring**: Renamed `Happy-learning` → `SwissKitJ-Plugin-HappyLearning`, moved plugins to `OfficalPlugin/` directory
- **Module Organization**: Added `SwissKitJ-Api` as a shared API module

---

## 1.0.0

**Release Date:** 2026-03-29

---

### Introduction

SwissKit is a modular desktop productivity toolkit built with Java Swing, designed to simplify daily office tasks. It provides powerful Excel processing capabilities, email management with mass-sending features, and a flexible plugin system for extensibility.

**Key Characteristics:**
- Cross-platform: Windows, Linux, macOS (Apple Silicon)
- Plugin Architecture: Auto-discovers and loads plugins via Java SPI
- Modern UI: FlatLaf Look and Feel
- Database: H2 embedded database with MyBatis ORM
- Requirements: Java 11 or higher

---

### Features

#### Excel Kit
- **Complex Split Mode**: Split Excel files with complex configurations
- **Configuration Editor**: Visual editor for split parameters
- **Progress Tracking**: Real-time progress bar with animation

#### Email Kit
- **Mass Email Sending**: Send emails to recipients based on tags
- **Address Book**: Manage contacts with tag associations
- **Tag Management**: Organize recipients by tags
- **Sent Log**: Track email sending history with status

#### Plugin System
- **Auto-Discovery**: Automatically finds plugins in `META-INF/services`
- **Isolated Loading**: Plugins loaded in isolated ClassLoader
- **Easy Extension**: Implement `KitPage` interface to add new tools

#### Settings
- **Unified Configuration**: Centralized settings management
- **Plugin Management**: View and manage installed plugins

---

### Installation

#### System Requirements
- Java 11 or higher (JDK/JRE)
- Windows 10+, Linux (glibc 2.17+), or macOS 11+

#### Windows Installation
1. Download `SwissKit-windows.zip`
2. Extract to desired location
3. Run `SwissKit.exe`

#### Linux Installation
1. Download `SwissKit-linux.zip`
2. Extract: `unzip SwissKit-linux.zip`
3. Run: `./run.sh`

#### macOS Installation
1. Download `SwissKit-macos-apple-silicon.zip`
2. Extract: `unzip SwissKit-macos-apple-silicon.zip`
3. Run: `open SwissKit.app` or `./run.sh`

**Note:** Java 11+ is required. Download from [Adoptium](https://adoptium.net/).

---

### For Developers

#### Build from Source

```bash
# Install API module to local Maven repo (required)
mvn install -f SwissKitJ-Api/pom.xml -DskipTests

# Compile
mvn clean compile

# Package executable JAR
mvn clean package -DskipTests

# Run (development)
mvn exec:java -Dexec.mainClass="fan.summer.Main"
```

#### Project Structure
```
SwissKitJ/
├── SwissKitJ-Api/                         # Shared API module (interfaces, annotations)
├── SwissKit/                              # Main application
├── OfficalPlugin/
│   ├── SwissKitJ-Plugin-HappyLearning/   # Auto-learning plugin
│   └── SwissKitJ-Plugin-Qcc/              # CSV to Excel conversion plugin
└── docs/                                  # Documentation
```

#### Plugin Development
1. Create class implementing `KitPage` interface
2. Annotate with `@SwissKitPage`
3. Register in `META-INF/services/fan.summer.api.KitPage`
4. Tool appears automatically in sidebar

#### API Dependency
```xml
<dependency>
    <groupId>fan.summer.api</groupId>
    <artifactId>SwissKitJ-Api</artifactId>
    <version>1.0.0</version>
</dependency>
```

---

### Changelog

The following is the complete version history.

---

## 1.0.0-RC.1

### 🔧 Fixes

- ExcelSplitWorker: Fix EDT violation by using GradientProgressBar type instead of JProgressBar for proper animation
- EmailSentWorker: Division by zero guard when totalEmails is zero
- EmailSentWorker: NPE guard when tagCollect returns null
- SetComplexSplitConfigWorker: EDT violation fix - read Swing component values in constructor
- MassSentConfigView: NPE guard for config.getToTag()
- EmailKitPage: NPE guard for config.getToTag()/getCcTag() in equals comparison
- QueryAllEmailInfoWorker: NPE guard when JSON.parseArray returns null
- ConfigEditorView: Add null-safe objToString() helper for table values
- PluginLoader: Check mkdirs() return value
- GradientProgressBar: Stop animation timer in removeNotify() to prevent resource leaks
- ShowConfigViewWorker: Re-throw exceptions instead of silent catch

### 🔄 Changes

- Maven: Disable resource filtering to prevent binary icon files from being corrupted

---

## 1.0.0-Beta.4

### 🚀 Enhancements

- Settings: Add plugin management UI with installed plugins table and uninstall functionality
- HappyLearning: Display current lesson ID and name (brief) during learning sessions
- HappyLearning: Add progress bar text display showing "current/max h" format

### 🔧 Fixes

- EDT violations: SetComplexSplitConfigWorker and ClearComplexSplitConfigWorker now properly wrap UI updates in SwingUtilities.invokeLater()
- Resource leaks: EmailSentWorker SqlSession usage restructured with try-with-resources
- Resource leaks: ExcelUtil Workbook leaks fixed on exception paths
- NullPointerExceptions: Fixed in EmailSentWorker (progressBar, emailTags), EmailKitPage (tagList), EmailTagsView (parseLong), Main.java (getCause())
- Silent failures: QueryAllEmailInfoWorker now returns null instead of empty list on error for proper error handling
- Cancellation support: EmailSentWorker mass-sending loop now checks isCancelled()

---

## 1.0.0-Beta.3

### 🚀 Enhancements

- Email Address Book: Add double-click editing functionality for contacts
- Email Address Book: Reset button now clears only tag information

---

## 1.0.0-Beta.2

### 🔧 Fixes

- PluginLoader: IsolatedPluginClassLoader now properly delegates JDK classes (java.*, javax.*, sun.*, com.sun.*) to main
  app ClassLoader
- PluginLoader: Universal classloading fallback ensures plugin DTOs and third-party library classes are accessible from
  isolated plugin JARs

### 📝 Documentation

- Add IsolatedPluginClassLoader classloading strategy to architecture.md
- Fix JAR filename typos: Bata.1.jar → Beta.2.jar in documentation
- Update table alignment and spacing in README.md and docs/

---

## 1.0.0-Beta.1

### 🚀 Enhancements

- Add error dialog display when email sending fails

### 🔧 Fixes

- Center email sent log table layout in viewing dialog

---

## 1.0.0-Alpha5

### 🚀 Enhancements

- Add SwissKitJ-Api module for shared components and plugin development
- Add email sent log viewing functionality with status tracking

### 🔄 Changes

- Move KitPage interface to SwissKitJ-Api module
- Move SwissKitPage annotation to SwissKitJ-Api module
- Move UI components (GradientProgressBar, FixedWidthComboBox) to SwissKitJ-Api module

---

## 1.0-Alpha4

### 🚀 Enhancements

- Add progress bar support for email sending
- Display tag names instead of IDs in configuration view
- Improve progress bar real-time updates

---

## 1.0-Alpha3

### 🔄 Changes

- Refactor tag association mechanism to use tag ID instead of tag name

---

## 1.0-Alpha2

### 🚀 Enhancements

- Add email sending functionality with SMTP support
- Add mass email sending feature with tag-based recipients
- Add attachment support by tag-based folder selection

---

## 1.0-Alpha

### 🚀 Enhancements

- Add Excel complex split mode with custom configuration
- Add email address book management feature
- Add email tag management feature
- Add plugin loading system
- Add custom UI components (GradientProgressBar, FixedWidthComboBox)

1	# Features
2	
3	SwissKit provides a variety of tools for everyday tasks. This section covers all available features and how to use them.
4	
5	## Table of Contents
6	
7	- [Excel Tool](#excel-tool)
8	- [Email Tool](#email-tool)
9	- [Settings](#settings)
10	- [Welcome Page](#welcome-page)
11	- [Plugin System](#plugin-system)
12	- [Custom UI Components](#custom-plugin.swisskit.hpl.ui-components)
13	
14	## Excel Tool
15	
16	The Excel tool provides powerful file processing capabilities including analysis and splitting.
17	
18	### File Analysis
19	
20	Analyze Excel files to extract structure and header information.
21	
22	**Features**:
23	
24	- Read all sheet pages
25	- Extract headers from each sheet
26	- Real-time progress display
27	- Support for .xlsx and .xls formats
28	
29	**How to Use**:
30	
31	1. Click the "Select File" button
32	2. Choose an Excel file
33	3. Click "Analyze" button
34	4. View results in the progress area
35	
36	**Example Output**:
37	
38	```
39	Parsing completed! Total 3 sheets
40	- Sheet1: [ID, Name, Email, Phone]
41	- Sheet2: [Date, Amount, Status]
42	- Sheet3: [Product, Quantity, Price]
43	```
44	
45	### File Splitting
46	
47	Split Excel files by sheet into separate files.
48	
49	**Features**:
50	
51	- Split each sheet into individual Excel file
52	- Real-time progress updates with percentage
53	- Automatic header preservation
54	- Warning dialogs for validation
55	
56	**How to Use**:
57	
58	1. Select an Excel file
59	2. Choose output directory
60	3. Select "Split by Sheet" checkbox
61	4. Click "Split" button
62	5. Wait for completion
63	
64	**Output Structure**:
65	
66	```
67	output/
68	├── Sheet1.xlsx
69	├── Sheet2.xlsx
70	└── Sheet3.xlsx
71	```
72	
73	### Complex Split Mode
74	
75	Advanced splitting with custom configuration stored in database.
76	
77	**Features**:
78	
79	- Custom header row index
80	- Custom split column index
81	- Configuration persistence
82	- Edit saved configurations
83	
84	**How to Use**:
85	
86	1. Select an Excel file
87	2. Analyze the file first
88	3. Configure header and column indices
89	4. Save configuration for reuse
90	5. View/Edit configurations via "View Config" button
91	
92	### Progress Tracking
93	
94	All Excel operations include real-time progress tracking:
95	
96	- Visual progress bar with gradient animation
97	- Percentage display
98	- Status messages
99	- Completion notifications
100	
101	## Email Tool
102	
103	The email tool allows you to compose and send emails, with support for single and mass email sending. SwissKit uses *
104	*Simple Java Mail** library for robust SMTP email handling.
105	
106	**Features**:
107	
108	- Email composition with subject and body
109	- **Rich text editor** with formatting toolbar (bold, italic, underline, font size, text color, alignment)
110	- Mass email mode with tag-based recipients
111	- SMTP configuration integration (via Simple Java Mail)
112	- Attachment support by tag-based folder selection
113	- Sent log viewing for tracking email history
114	
115	### Single Email Sending
116	
117	Send emails to individual recipients.
118	
119	**How to Use**:
120	
121	1. Enter email subject
122	2. Select recipient tags (To/Cc)
123	3. Enter email body content
124	4. Click "Sent" button
125	5. Wait for sending completion
126	
127	### Mass Email Sending
128	
129	Send emails to multiple recipients based on tag configuration.
130	
131	**Features**:
132	
133	- Tag-based recipient selection
134	- Attachment files from tag-based folders
135	- Automatic recipient matching by tags
136	- Support for multiple tags (To/Cc)
137	- **Real-time progress bar** showing sending progress
138	- Completion status display
139	
140	**How to Use**:
141	
142	1. Enable "MassSent" checkbox
143	2. Click "MassSendConfig" to configure
144	3. Select To tag (required)
145	4. Select Cc tag (optional)
146	5. Enable attachment if needed, select folder
147	6. Save configuration
148	7. Click "Sent" to send emails
149	
150	**How It Works**:
151	
152	1. The system generates a unique task ID for the mass sending session
153	2. Load all contacts from address book
154	3. Parse attachment files from selected folder (filename format: `filename_tag.ext`)
155	4. For each tag, find matching recipients and send emails
156	5. Show progress and results
157	6. Log all sent emails to database for history tracking
158	
159	### View Configuration
160	
161	View the current mass sending configuration.
162	
163	**How to Use**:
164	
165	1. Enable "MassSent" checkbox
166	2. Click "ViewSentConfig" button
167	3. View configuration in dialog
168	
169	### View Sent Log
170	
171	View the history of all sent emails with status tracking.
172	
173	**Features**:
174	
175	- View all sent emails in a table
176	- Track email status (success/failure)
177	- View recipients (To, Cc, Bcc)
178	- View subject, content, and attachments
179	- View send timestamp
180	
181	**How to Use**:
182	
183	1. Click "ViewSentLog" button on Email Tool page
184	2. A dialog will appear with all sent email records
185	3. Review email details including:
186	    - Subject
187	    - Recipients (To, Cc, Bcc)
188	    - Content
189	    - Attachments
190	    - Send Time
191	    - Success Status
192	
193	**Table Columns**:
194	
195	| Column     | Description                             |
196	|------------|-----------------------------------------|
197	| ID         | Record identifier                       |
198	| Subject    | Email subject line                      |
199	| To         | Primary recipients                      |
200	| Cc         | Carbon copy recipients                  |
201	| Bcc        | Blind carbon copy recipients            |
202	| Content    | Email body content                      |
203	| Attachment | Attached files                          |
204	| Send Time  | Timestamp when email was sent           |
205	| Success    | Whether the email was sent successfully |
206	
207	## Settings
208	
209	The settings page provides application configuration options with multiple tabs.
210	
211	### Email Settings Tab
212	
213	Configure SMTP server for email sending.
214	
215	**Features**:
216	
217	- Protocol selection (SMTP)
218	- Server URL and port configuration
219	- Username and password
220	- TLS/SSL support
221	- Test email sending
222	
223	### Email Address Book Tab
224	
225	Manage email contacts with nicknames and tags.
226	
227	**Features**:
228	
229	- Add new email addresses
230	- Edit existing contacts by double-clicking a row
231	- Assign tags to contacts
232	- View all contacts in table format
233	
234	**How to Use**:
235	
236	1. Click "Open Address Book" button
237	2. View existing contacts in the table
238	3. Click "Add New Address" to add a contact
239	4. Enter email, nickname, and select tags
240	5. Click "Insert" to save
241	6. Double-click any row to edit the contact information
242	
243	### Tag Management
244	
245	Create and manage tags for categorizing contacts.
246	
247	**Features**:
248	
249	- Create new tags
250	- Edit existing tags
251	- Delete tags
252	- Tags stored in database
253	
254	**How to Use**:
255	
256	1. Open Address Book
257	2. Click "Modify Tags" button
258	3. Add new tag or double-click to edit
259	4. Tags are automatically available in Add Address dialog
260	
261	### Plugin Tab
262	
263	Install external JAR plugins to extend functionality.
264	
265	**Features**:
266	
267	- Select JAR file from file system
268	- Install plugins to `.swisskit/plugins/` directory
269	- Plugins loaded on next startup
270	
271	**How to Use**:
272	
273	1. Navigate to Plugin tab
274	2. Click "Choice Plugin" to select a JAR file
275	3. Click "Upload" to install
276	4. Restart application to load the new plugin
277	
278	## Welcome Page
279	
280	The welcome page provides an overview of the application.
281	
282	**Features**:
283	
284	- Application introduction
285	- Quick access to all tools
286	- Operation guidance
287	- Modern, clean design
288	
289	## Plugin System
290	
291	SwissKit's modular architecture allows easy extension of functionality using SPI (Service Provider Interface).
292	
293	### Adding New Tools
294	
295	To add a new tool:
296	
297	1. Create a package under `fan.summer.kitpage`
298	2. Implement the `KitPage` interface (from `fan.summer.api.KitPage`)
299	3. Add `@SwissKitPage` annotation (from `fan.summer.annoattion.SwissKitPage`)
300	4. Register in SPI plugin.swisskit.hpl.service file (`META-INF/services/fan.summer.api.KitPage`)
301	5. The tool will be automatically discovered and sorted by order
302	
303	**Example**:
304	
305	```java
306	import fan.summer.api.KitPage;
307	import fan.summer.annoattion.SwissKitPage;
308	
309	@SwissKitPage(
310	        menuName = "🔧 My Tool",
311	        menuTooltip = "Open My Tool",
312	        visible = true,
313	        order = 10
314	)
315	public class MyToolPage implements KitPage {
316	    private JPanel panel;
317	
318	    public MyToolPage() {
319	        initComponents();
320	    }
321	
322	    @Override
323	    public JPanel getPanel() {
324	        return panel;
325	    }
326	}
327	```
328	
329	### Installing External Plugins
330	
331	1. Build your plugin as a JAR file with `SwissKitJ-Api` as dependency
332	2. Open Settings → Plugin tab
333	3. Select and upload the JAR file
334	4. Restart SwissKit
335	
336	## HappyLearning Plugin
337	
338	The HappyLearning plugin provides automated online learning functionality with real-time progress tracking.
339	
340	**Features**:
341	
342	- **Passkey Configuration** - Set authentication passkey for learning service
343	- **Config Upload** - Upload learning configuration files (netschool-headers.json)
344	- **Auto Learning** - Automated learning with configurable lesson type
345	- **Progress Tracking** - Real-time progress bars for major and elective subjects
346	- **Start/Stop Control** - Manual control over learning sessions with cancel support
347	- **Background Processing** - Non-blocking UI using SwingWorker with periodic progress polling
348	
349	**How to Use**:
350	
351	1. Upload configuration file (netschool-headers.json) via "Upload Config" button
352	2. Enter passkey in the text field
353	3. Click "Set PassKey" to save authentication credentials
354	4. Select lesson type (Auto or specific type)
355	5. Click "Start" to begin automated learning
356	6. Monitor progress via the dual progress bars (major and elective subjects)
357	7. Click "Stop" to cancel learning at any time
358	
359	**Architecture**:
360	
361	- `HappyLearning` - Main UI page implementing `KitPage`
362	- `HappyLearningService` - Service layer for API calls
363	- `HappyLearningWorker` - SwingWorker for background learning with progress updates
364	- `WebUtil` - HTTP client with gzip decompression support
365	- `ConfigLoader` - JSON configuration file loader
366	
367	## Database Layer
368	
369	SwissKit includes a built-in database layer using H2 and MyBatis for persistent storage.
370	
371	### Features
372	
373	- **Embedded Database** - No external server required
374	- **Automatic Initialization** - Database created on first run
375	- **MyBatis Integration** - Clean DAO layer with XML mappers
376	- **Multiple Tables** - Support for various data types
377	
378	### Database Location
379	
380	- **Path**: `.swisskit/swisskit.db`
381	- **Auto-created**: On first application run
382	
383	### Current Tables
384	
385	| Table                     | Purpose                                    |
386	|---------------------------|--------------------------------------------|
387	| `swiss_kit_setting_email` | Email SMTP configuration                   |
388	| `complex_split_config`    | Excel complex split settings               |
389	| `email_address_book`      | Email contacts with nicknames and tags     |
390	| `email_tag`               | Tags for categorizing contacts             |
391	| `email_mass_sent_config`  | Mass email sending configuration           |
392	| `email_sent_log`          | Email sending history with status tracking |
393	
394	## Custom UI Components
395	
396	SwissKit includes custom UI components for enhanced user experience. These components are available in the
397	`SwissKitJ-Api` module.
398	
399	### GradientProgressBar
400	
401	A progress bar with smooth animation and gradient effects.
402	
403	**Features**:
404	
405	- Blue to purple gradient
406	- Smooth easing animation
407	- Rounded corners
408	- Glossy highlight effect
409	- Optional text display
410	- 60 FPS animation
411	
412	**Usage**:
413	
414	```java
415	GradientProgressBar progressBar = new GradientProgressBar();
416	progressBar.setMinimum(0);
417	progressBar.setMaximum(100);
418	progressBar.setValue(50);
419	progressBar.setStringPainted(true);
420	progressBar.setString("Processing... 50%");
421	```
422	
423	### FixedWidthComboBox
424	
425	A combo box with fixed width for consistent UI layout.
426	
427	**Features**:
428	
429	- Fixed width configuration
430	- Consistent appearance
431	- Easy integration
432	
433	**Usage**:
434	
435	```java
436	FixedWidthComboBox comboBox = new FixedWidthComboBox(200);
437	comboBox.addItem("Option 1");
438	comboBox.addItem("Option 2");
439	```
440	
441	### SideMenuBar
442	
443	Dynamic side menu component with modern styling.
444	
445	**Features**:
446	
447	- 160px width
448	- Selected state highlighting
449	- Mouse hover effects
450	- Custom icons and tooltips
451	- Runtime page management
452	- Automatic page discovery
453	
454	**Color Scheme**:
455	
456	- Selected background: Dark gray (#2D2D2D)
457	- Selected text: Purple (#BB86FC)
458	- Hover background: Light gray (#E8E8E8)
459	- Default background: Lighter gray (#F3F3F3)
460	
461	### TableUtil
462	
463	Utility class for consistent JTable initialization.
464	
465	**Features**:
466	
467	- Easy table setup with column names and data
468	- Configurable cell editability
469	- Simple API
470	
471	**Usage**:
472	
473	```java
474	// Initialize table with non-editable cells
475	TableUtil.initTable(table, columns, rowData, 99);
476	```
477	
478	## Performance Features
479	
480	SwissKit is optimized for performance:
481	
482	- **Streaming Excel Processing** - Uses Apache FESOD for efficient memory usage
483	- **Async Operations** - Background tasks with SwingWorker
484	- **Progress Tracking** - Real-time updates without blocking UI
485	- **Lazy Loading** - Pages loaded on demand
486	
487	## Accessibility
488	
489	SwissKit aims to be accessible:
490	
491	- Keyboard navigation support
492	- Clear visual feedback
493	- Consistent UI patterns
494	- Descriptive tooltips
495	
496	## Future Features
497	
498	Planned enhancements:
499	
500	- [ ] PDF processing tool
501	- [ ] Image processing tool
502	- [x] Theme switching
503	- [x] Multi-language support
504	
505	---
506	
507	**Want to learn more?** Check out the [Architecture](architecture.md) section to understand how everything works.

# Features

SwissKit provides a variety of tools for everyday tasks. This section covers all available features and how to use them.

## Excel Tool

The Excel tool provides powerful file processing capabilities including analysis and splitting.

### File Analysis

Analyze Excel files to extract structure and header information.

**Features**:

- Read all sheet pages
- Extract headers from each sheet
- Real-time progress display
- Support for .xlsx and .xls formats

**How to Use**:

1. Click the "Select File" button
2. Choose an Excel file
3. Click "Analyze" button
4. View results in the progress area

**Example Output**:

```
Parsing completed! Total 3 sheets
- Sheet1: [ID, Name, Email, Phone]
- Sheet2: [Date, Amount, Status]
- Sheet3: [Product, Quantity, Price]
```

### File Splitting

Split Excel files by sheet into separate files.

**Features**:

- Split each sheet into individual Excel file
- Real-time progress updates with percentage
- Automatic header preservation
- Warning dialogs for validation

**How to Use**:

1. Select an Excel file
2. Choose output directory
3. Select "Split by Sheet" checkbox
4. Click "Split" button
5. Wait for completion

**Output Structure**:

```
output/
├── Sheet1.xlsx
├── Sheet2.xlsx
└── Sheet3.xlsx
```

### Complex Split Mode

Advanced splitting with custom configuration stored in database.

**Features**:

- Custom header row index
- Custom split column index
- Configuration persistence
- Edit saved configurations

**How to Use**:

1. Select an Excel file
2. Analyze the file first
3. Configure header and column indices
4. Save configuration for reuse
5. View/Edit configurations via "View Config" button

### Progress Tracking

All Excel operations include real-time progress tracking:

- Visual progress bar with gradient animation
- Percentage display
- Status messages
- Completion notifications

## Email Tool

The email tool allows you to compose and send emails, with support for single and mass email sending. SwissKit uses *
*Simple Java Mail** library for robust SMTP email handling.

**Features**:

- Email composition with subject and body
- **Rich text editor** with formatting toolbar (bold, italic, underline, font size, text color, alignment)
- Mass email mode with tag-based recipients
- SMTP configuration integration (via Simple Java Mail)
- Attachment support by tag-based folder selection
- Sent log viewing for tracking email history

### Single Email Sending

Send emails to individual recipients.

**How to Use**:

1. Enter email subject
2. Select recipient tags (To/Cc)
3. Enter email body content
4. Click "Sent" button
5. Wait for sending completion

### Mass Email Sending

Send emails to multiple recipients based on tag configuration.

**Features**:

- Tag-based recipient selection
- Attachment files from tag-based folders
- Automatic recipient matching by tags
- Support for multiple tags (To/Cc)
- **Real-time progress bar** showing sending progress
- Completion status display

**How to Use**:

1. Enable "MassSent" checkbox
2. Click "MassSendConfig" to configure
3. Select To tag (required)
4. Select Cc tag (optional)
5. Enable attachment if needed, select folder
6. Save configuration
7. Click "Sent" to send emails

**How It Works**:

1. The system generates a unique task ID for the mass sending session
2. Load all contacts from address book
3. Parse attachment files from selected folder (filename format: `filename_tag.ext`)
4. For each tag, find matching recipients and send emails
5. Show progress and results
6. Log all sent emails to database for history tracking

### View Configuration

View the current mass sending configuration.

**How to Use**:

1. Enable "MassSent" checkbox
2. Click "ViewSentConfig" button
3. View configuration in dialog

### View Sent Log

View the history of all sent emails with status tracking.

**Features**:

- View all sent emails in a table
- Track email status (success/failure)
- View recipients (To, Cc, Bcc)
- View subject, content, and attachments
- View send timestamp

**How to Use**:

1. Click "ViewSentLog" button on Email Tool page
2. A dialog will appear with all sent email records
3. Review email details including:
    - Subject
    - Recipients (To, Cc, Bcc)
    - Content
    - Attachments
    - Send Time
    - Success Status

**Table Columns**:

| Column     | Description                             |
|------------|-----------------------------------------|
| ID         | Record identifier                       |
| Subject    | Email subject line                      |
| To         | Primary recipients                      |
| Cc         | Carbon copy recipients                  |
| Bcc        | Blind carbon copy recipients            |
| Content    | Email body content                      |
| Attachment | Attached files                          |
| Send Time  | Timestamp when email was sent           |
| Success    | Whether the email was sent successfully |

## Settings

The settings page provides application configuration options with multiple tabs.

### Email Settings Tab

Configure SMTP server for email sending.

**Features**:

- Protocol selection (SMTP)
- Server URL and port configuration
- Username and password
- TLS/SSL support
- Test email sending

### Email Address Book Tab

Manage email contacts with nicknames and tags.

**Features**:

- Add new email addresses
- Edit existing contacts by double-clicking a row
- Assign tags to contacts
- View all contacts in table format

**How to Use**:

1. Click "Open Address Book" button
2. View existing contacts in the table
3. Click "Add New Address" to add a contact
4. Enter email, nickname, and select tags
5. Click "Insert" to save
6. Double-click any row to edit the contact information

### Tag Management

Create and manage tags for categorizing contacts.

**Features**:

- Create new tags
- Edit existing tags
- Delete tags
- Tags stored in database

**How to Use**:

1. Open Address Book
2. Click "Modify Tags" button
3. Add new tag or double-click to edit
4. Tags are automatically available in Add Address dialog

### Plugin Tab

Install external JAR plugins to extend functionality.

**Features**:

- Select JAR file from file system
- Install plugins to `.swisskit/plugins/` directory
- Plugins loaded on next startup

**How to Use**:

1. Navigate to Plugin tab
2. Click "Choice Plugin" to select a JAR file
3. Click "Upload" to install
4. Restart application to load the new plugin

## Welcome Page

The welcome page provides an overview of the application.

**Features**:

- Application introduction
- Quick access to all tools
- Operation guidance
- Modern, clean design

## Plugin System

SwissKit's modular architecture allows easy extension of functionality using SPI (Service Provider Interface).

### Adding New Tools

To add a new tool:

1. Create a package under `fan.summer.kitpage`
2. Implement the `KitPage` interface (from `fan.summer.api.KitPage`)
3. Add `@SwissKitPage` annotation (from `fan.summer.annoattion.SwissKitPage`)
4. Register in SPI plugin.swisskit.hpl.service file (`META-INF/services/fan.summer.api.KitPage`)
5. The tool will be automatically discovered and sorted by order

**Example**:

```java
import fan.summer.api.KitPage;
import fan.summer.annoattion.SwissKitPage;

@SwissKitPage(
        menuName = "🔧 My Tool",
        menuTooltip = "Open My Tool",
        visible = true,
        order = 10
)
public class MyToolPage implements KitPage {
    private JPanel panel;

    public MyToolPage() {
        initComponents();
    }

    @Override
    public JPanel getPanel() {
        return panel;
    }
}
```

### Installing External Plugins

1. Build your plugin as a JAR file with `SwissKitJ-Api` as dependency
2. Open Settings → Plugin tab
3. Select and upload the JAR file
4. Restart SwissKit

## HappyLearning Plugin

The HappyLearning plugin provides automated online learning functionality with real-time progress tracking.

**Features**:

- **Passkey Configuration** - Set authentication passkey for learning service
- **Config Upload** - Upload learning configuration files (netschool-headers.json)
- **Auto Learning** - Automated learning with configurable lesson type
- **Progress Tracking** - Real-time progress bars for major and elective subjects
- **Start/Stop Control** - Manual control over learning sessions with cancel support
- **Background Processing** - Non-blocking UI using SwingWorker with periodic progress polling

**How to Use**:

1. Upload configuration file (netschool-headers.json) via "Upload Config" button
2. Enter passkey in the text field
3. Click "Set PassKey" to save authentication credentials
4. Select lesson type (Auto or specific type)
5. Click "Start" to begin automated learning
6. Monitor progress via the dual progress bars (major and elective subjects)
7. Click "Stop" to cancel learning at any time

**Architecture**:

- `HappyLearning` - Main UI page implementing `KitPage`
- `HappyLearningService` - Service layer for API calls
- `HappyLearningWorker` - SwingWorker for background learning with progress updates
- `WebUtil` - HTTP client with gzip decompression support
- `ConfigLoader` - JSON configuration file loader

## Database Layer

SwissKit includes a built-in database layer using H2 and MyBatis for persistent storage.

### Features

- **Embedded Database** - No external server required
- **Automatic Initialization** - Database created on first run
- **MyBatis Integration** - Clean DAO layer with XML mappers
- **Multiple Tables** - Support for various data types

### Database Location

- **Path**: `.swisskit/swisskit.db`
- **Auto-created**: On first application run

### Current Tables

| Table                     | Purpose                                    |
|---------------------------|--------------------------------------------|
| `swiss_kit_setting_email` | Email SMTP configuration                   |
| `complex_split_config`    | Excel complex split settings               |
| `email_address_book`      | Email contacts with nicknames and tags     |
| `email_tag`               | Tags for categorizing contacts             |
| `email_mass_sent_config`  | Mass email sending configuration           |
| `email_sent_log`          | Email sending history with status tracking |

## Custom UI Components

SwissKit includes custom UI components for enhanced user experience. These components are available in the
`SwissKitJ-Api` module.

### GradientProgressBar

A progress bar with smooth animation and gradient effects.

**Features**:

- Blue to purple gradient
- Smooth easing animation
- Rounded corners
- Glossy highlight effect
- Optional text display
- 60 FPS animation

**Usage**:

```java
GradientProgressBar progressBar = new GradientProgressBar();
progressBar.setMinimum(0);
progressBar.setMaximum(100);
progressBar.setValue(50);
progressBar.setStringPainted(true);
progressBar.setString("Processing... 50%");
```

### FixedWidthComboBox

A combo box with fixed width for consistent UI layout.

**Features**:

- Fixed width configuration
- Consistent appearance
- Easy integration

**Usage**:

```java
FixedWidthComboBox comboBox = new FixedWidthComboBox(200);
comboBox.addItem("Option 1");
comboBox.addItem("Option 2");
```

### SideMenuBar

Dynamic side menu component with modern styling.

**Features**:

- 160px width
- Selected state highlighting
- Mouse hover effects
- Custom icons and tooltips
- Runtime page management
- Automatic page discovery

**Color Scheme**:

- Selected background: Dark gray (#2D2D2D)
- Selected text: Purple (#BB86FC)
- Hover background: Light gray (#E8E8E8)
- Default background: Lighter gray (#F3F3F3)

### TableUtil

Utility class for consistent JTable initialization.

**Features**:

- Easy table setup with column names and data
- Configurable cell editability
- Simple API

**Usage**:

```java
// Initialize table with non-editable cells
TableUtil.initTable(table, columns, rowData, 99);
```

## Performance Features

SwissKit is optimized for performance:

- **Streaming Excel Processing** - Uses Apache FESOD for efficient memory usage
- **Async Operations** - Background tasks with SwingWorker
- **Progress Tracking** - Real-time updates without blocking UI
- **Lazy Loading** - Pages loaded on demand

## Accessibility

SwissKit aims to be accessible:

- Keyboard navigation support
- Clear visual feedback
- Consistent UI patterns
- Descriptive tooltips

## Future Features

Planned enhancements:

- [ ] PDF processing tool
- [ ] Image processing tool
- [x] Theme switching
- [x] Multi-language support

---

**Want to learn more?** Check out the [Architecture](architecture.md) section to understand how everything works.

file.content:
# Getting Started

Welcome to SwissKit! This guide will help you install, configure, and run SwissKit on your system.

## Requirements

Before you begin, ensure you have the following installed:

- **JDK 11 or higher**
- **Maven 3.6 or higher** (required for building from source)

### Verify Installation

```bash
# Check Java version
java -version

# Check Maven version
mvn -version
```

## Installation

### Option 1: Download Pre-built JAR

The easiest way to get started is to download the pre-built JAR file from the [GitHub Releases](https://github.com/MuskStark/SwissKitJ/releases) page.

1. Download `SwissKit-2.1.1.jar`
2. Run the application:
   ```bash
   java -jar SwissKit-2.1.1.jar
   ```

### Option 2: Build from Source

If you want to build from source, follow these steps:

```bash
# Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/MuskStark/SwissKitJ.git
cd SwissKitJ

# Install API module first (required)
mvn install -f SwissKitJ-Api/pom.xml -DskipTests

# Build the main project
mvn clean package

# Run the application
java -jar target/SwissKit-2.1.1.jar
```

## Configuration

### Application Settings

SwissKit comes with sensible defaults, but you can customize certain aspects:

#### Window Settings

Default window settings:
- **Minimum Size**: 800x500 pixels
- **Default Size**: 900x600 pixels
- **Theme**: FlatIntelliJLaf

#### Custom Icon

Place an icon file in `src/main/resources/`:
- `icon.png` (preferred)
- `icon.jpg`
- `app.png`

If not found, a log message will be displayed.

## Running the Application

### Using Maven Exec Plugin

```bash
mvn exec:java -Dexec.mainClass="fan.summer.Main"
```

### Using JAR File

```bash
java -jar target/SwissKit-2.0.2.jar
```

### Using IDE

If you're using IntelliJ IDEA:

1. Open the project
2. Locate `Main.java` in `src/main/java/fan/summer/`
3. Right-click and select "Run 'Main.main()'"

## First Steps

Once SwissKit is running:

1. **Splash Screen** - You'll see a loading screen while the application initializes
2. **Welcome Page** - After loading, you'll see the welcome page with an overview
3. **Select a Tool** - Choose a tool from the left sidebar:
   - **Excel Tool** - Process and split Excel files
   - **Email Tool** - Compose and send emails, view sent history
4. **Explore** - Click around to explore the available features

## Troubleshooting

### Application Won't Start

**Issue**: Application fails to start

**Solution**:
- Ensure JDK 11+ is installed
- Check `JAVA_HOME` environment variable
- Verify you're using the correct Java version

```bash
# Check Java version
java -version
```

### Excel Files Not Reading

**Issue**: Excel files are not being read properly

**Solution**:
- Ensure file format is supported (.xlsx, .xls)
- Check file permissions
- Verify the file is not corrupted

### UI Not Rendering Correctly

**Issue**: User interface appears broken or misaligned

**Solution**:
- Update FlatLaf dependencies
- Rebuild the project
- Try running with a fresh build

```bash
mvn clean package
```

### API Module Not Found

**Issue**: Build fails with "SwissKitJ-Api not found"

**Solution**:
- Install the API module first:
  ```bash
  mvn install -f SwissKitJ-Api/pom.xml -DskipTests
  ```

## Next Steps

Now that you have SwissKit running, you can:

- [Explore Features](features.md) - Learn about available tools
- [Read Architecture](architecture.md) - Understand how it works
- [Start Developing](development.md) - Contribute to the project

## Getting Help

If you encounter any issues:

1. Check the [Troubleshooting](#troubleshooting) section above
2. Search existing [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/MuskStark/SwissKitJ/issues)
3. Create a new issue with details about your problem

---

**Ready to explore?** Continue to [Features](features.md) to learn what SwissKit can do!
