1	# Development Guide
2	
3	This guide covers everything you need to know to contribute to SwissKit, from setting up your development environment to
4	implementing new features.
5	
6	## Table of Contents
7	
8	- [Prerequisites](#prerequisites)
9	- [Setting Up Development Environment](#setting-up-development-environment)
10	- [Project Structure](#project-structure)
11	- [Code Standards](#code-standards)
12	- [Adding New Tools](#adding-new-tools)
13	- [Testing](#testing)
14	- [Building](#building)
15	- [Common Tasks](#common-tasks)
16	
17	## Prerequisites
18	
19	Before you start developing, ensure you have:
20	
21	- **JDK 11 or higher**
22	- **Maven 3.6 or higher**
23	- **IntelliJ IDEA** (recommended) or your preferred IDE
24	- **Git**
25	
26	### Verify Installation
27	
28	```bash
29	# Check Java version
30	java -version
31	
32	# Check Maven version
33	mvn -version
34	
35	# Check Git version
36	git --version
37	```
38	
39	## Setting Up Development Environment
40	
41	### 1. Clone the Repository
42	
43	```bash
44	git clone https://github.com/MuskStark/SwissKitJ.git
45	cd SwissKit
46	```
47	
48	### 2. Import into IDE
49	
50	**IntelliJ IDEA**:
51	
52	1. Open IntelliJ IDEA
53	2. Select "Open" and choose the project directory
54	3. Wait for Maven to download dependencies
55	4. The project will be automatically recognized as a Maven project
56	
57	### 3. Build the Project
58	
59	```bash
60	mvn clean compile
61	```
62	
63	### 4. Run the Application
64	
65	```bash
66	mvn exec:java -Dexec.mainClass="fan.summer.Main"
67	```
68	
69	Or run `Main.java` directly from your IDE.
70	
71	## Project Structure
72	
73	```
74	SwissKit/
75	├── src/main/java/fan/summer/
76	│   ├── Main.java                    # Entry point
77	│   ├── annoattion/                  # Annotations
78	│   │   └── SwissKitPage.java       # Page annotation
79	│   ├── kitpage/                     # Tool modules
80	│   │   ├── KitPage.java            # Interface
81	│   │   ├── KitPageScanner.java     # Auto-discovery scanner
82	│   │   ├── WelcomePage.java        # Welcome page
83	│   │   ├── excel/                  # Excel tool
84	│   │   │   ├── listener/           # Event listeners
85	│   │   │   └── plugin.swisskit.hpl.worker/             # Background workers
86	│   │   └── email/                  # Email tool
87	│   ├── plugin.swisskit.hpl.ui/                         # Custom UI components
88	│   │   └── components/
89	│   └── utils/                      # Utilities
90	├── src/main/resources/              # Resources (icons, etc.)
91	├── pom.xml                          # Maven configuration
92	└── docs/                            # Documentation
93	```
94	
95	## Code Standards
96	
97	### Naming Conventions
98	
99	**Packages**:
100	
101	- Tool pages: `fan.summer.kitpage.{toolName}`
102	- Listeners: `listener/{function}Listener.java`
103	- Workers: `plugin.swisskit.hpl.worker/{function}Worker.java`
104	- Callbacks: `plugin.swisskit.hpl.worker/{function}Callback.java`
105	
106	**Classes**:
107	
108	- Pages: `{ToolName}KitPage` or `{ToolName}Page`
109	- Listeners: `{Function}Listener`
110	- Workers: `{Function}Worker`
111	- Callbacks: `{Function}Callback`
112	
113	**Methods and Variables**:
114	
115	- Use camelCase
116	- Be descriptive and concise
117	
118	```java
119	// Good
120	public void analyzeExcelFile(Path filePath) { }
121	private int currentProgress;
122	
123	// Bad
124	public void analyzeExcel() { }
125	private int cp;
126	```
127	
128	### Documentation
129	
130	- Add Javadoc for all public methods
131	- Use clear, concise descriptions
132	- Include parameters and return values
133	
134	```java
135	/**
136	 * Analyzes an Excel file and extracts header information.
137	 *
138	 * @param filePath the path to the Excel file to analyze
139	 * @return a map of sheet names to their headers
140	 * @throws IOException if the file cannot be read
141	 */
142	public Map<String, Map<Integer, String>> analyzeExcel(Path filePath) throws IOException {
143	    // Implementation
144	}
145	```
146	
147	### Language
148	
149	- All code comments must be in **English**
150	- All UI text must be in **English**
151	- Variable and method names in English
152	
153	### UI Standards
154	
155	- Use `SansSerif` font for UI components
156	- Use color constants from `UIUtils`
157	- Recommend layouts: `BorderLayout`, `GridBagLayout`, `BoxLayout`
158	
159	```java
160	JLabel titleLabel = new JLabel("My Tool");
161	titleLabel.setFont(new Font("SansSerif", Font.BOLD, 18));
162	panel.add(titleLabel, BorderLayout.NORTH);
163	```
164	
165	## Adding New Tools
166	
167	### Step-by-Step Guide
168	
169	**1. Create Package Structure**
170	
171	```bash
172	mkdir -p src/main/java/fan/summer/kitpage/mytool
173	```
174	
175	**2. Implement KitPage Interface with @SwissKitPage Annotation**
176	
177	```java
178	package fan.summer.kitpage.mytool;
179	
180	import fan.summer.api.KitPage;
181	import fan.summer.annoattion.SwissKitPage;
182	
183	import javax.swing.*;
184	import java.awt.*;
185	
186	@SwissKitPage(
187	        menuName = "🔧 My Tool",
188	        menuTooltip = "Open My Tool",
189	        visible = true,
190	        order = 10
191	)
192	public class MyToolPage implements KitPage {
193	    private JPanel panel;
194	
195	    public MyToolPage() {
196	        initComponents();
197	    }
198	
199	    private void initComponents() {
200	        panel = new JPanel(new BorderLayout());
201	
202	        // Add your UI components here
203	        JLabel titleLabel = new JLabel("My Tool");
204	        titleLabel.setFont(new Font("SansSerif", Font.BOLD, 18));
205	
206	        panel.add(titleLabel, BorderLayout.NORTH);
207	    }
208	
209	    @Override
210	    public JPanel getPanel() {
211	        return panel;
212	    }
213	}
214	```
215	
216	**Annotation Properties**:
217	
218	| Property      | Type    | Default | Description                   |
219	|---------------|---------|---------|-------------------------------|
220	| `menuName`    | String  | ""      | Display name in sidebar       |
221	| `menuTooltip` | String  | ""      | Tooltip on hover              |
222	| `visible`     | boolean | true    | Whether to show in menu       |
223	| `order`       | int     | 0       | Display order (lower = first) |
224	
225	**3. Build and Test**
226	
227	```bash
228	mvn clean compile
229	mvn exec:java -Dexec.mainClass="fan.summer.Main"
230	```
231	
232	Your new tool will appear automatically in the sidebar!
233	
234	### Adding Background Processing
235	
236	For time-consuming operations, use SwingWorker:
237	
238	```java
239	public class MyToolPage implements KitPage {
240	    private JProgressBar progressBar;
241	    private JButton processButton;
242	
243	    public MyToolPage() {
244	        initComponents();
245	    }
246	
247	    private void initComponents() {
248	        panel = new JPanel(new BorderLayout());
249	
250	        // Create components
251	        progressBar = new GradientProgressBar();
252	        processButton = new JButton("Process");
253	
254	        // Add action listener
255	        processButton.addActionListener(e -> {
256	            new MyWorker(progressBar, processButton, panel).execute();
257	        });
258	
259	        // Layout
260	        panel.add(progressBar, BorderLayout.CENTER);
261	        panel.add(processButton, BorderLayout.SOUTH);
262	    }
263	}
264	
265	class MyWorker extends SwingWorker<Void, Integer> {
266	    private final JProgressBar progressBar;
267	    private final JButton button;
268	    private final Component parent;
269	
270	    public MyWorker(JProgressBar progressBar, JButton button, Component parent) {
271	        this.progressBar = progressBar;
272	        this.button = button;
273	        this.parent = parent;
274	    }
275	
276	    @Override
277	    protected Void doInBackground() throws Exception {
278	        SwingUtilities.invokeLater(() -> {
279	            progressBar.setValue(0);
280	            progressBar.setStringPainted(true);
281	            button.setEnabled(false);
282	        });
283	
284	        // Do work here
285	        for (int i = 0; i <= 100; i++) {
286	            Thread.sleep(50);
287	            publish(i);
288	        }
289	
290	        return null;
291	    }
292	
293	    @Override
294	    protected void process(List<Integer> chunks) {
295	        if (!chunks.isEmpty()) {
296	            int latest = chunks.get(chunks.size() - 1);
297	            progressBar.setValue(latest);
298	            progressBar.setString("Processing... " + latest + "%");
299	        }
300	    }
301	
302	    @Override
303	    protected void done() {
304	        try {
305	            get();
306	            progressBar.setValue(100);
307	            progressBar.setString("Completed!");
308	            button.setEnabled(true);
309	        } catch (Exception e) {
310	            progressBar.setString("Failed: " + e.getMessage());
311	            JOptionPane.showMessageDialog(parent, e.getMessage(), "Error", JOptionPane.ERROR_MESSAGE);
312	            button.setEnabled(true);
313	        }
314	    }
315	}
316	```

371	### Adding a Dependency
372

397	### Debugging
398	
399	Enable verbose logging:
400	
401	```bash
402	java -Dlog4j.configurationFile=path/to/log4j2.xml -jar target/SwissKit-1.1.0.jar
403	```
404	
405	Or add logging to your code:
406	
407	```java
408	private static final Logger logger = LoggerFactory.getLogger(MyClass.class);
409	
410	logger.info("Processing started");
411	logger.error("Error occurred", exception);
412	```
413	
414	## Best Practices
415	
416	### Thread Safety
417	
418	- Always update UI components in EDT thread
419	- Use `SwingUtilities.invokeLater()` for UI updates from background threads
420	
421	```java
422	// Wrong - from background thread
423	progressBar.setValue(50);
424	
425	// Correct
426	SwingUtilities.invokeLater(() -> progressBar.setValue(50));
427	```
428	
429	### Error Handling
430	
431	- Always handle exceptions gracefully
432	- Provide user-friendly error messages
433	- Log errors for debugging
434	
435	```java
436	try {
437	    // Operation
438	} catch (Exception e) {
439	    logger.error("Operation failed", e);
440	    JOptionPane.showMessageDialog(parent,
441	            "Operation failed: " + e.getMessage(),
442	            "Error", JOptionPane.ERROR_MESSAGE);
443	}
444	```
445	
446	### Resource Management
447	
448	- Use try-with-resources for file operations
449	- Close resources properly
450	
451	```java
452	try (Workbook workbook = WorkbookFactory.create(file)) {
453	    // Use workbook
454	} // Automatically closed
455	```

file.content:
# Architecture

SwissKit is built with a modular, plugin-based architecture that allows for easy extension and maintenance. This section
covers the core architectural patterns and design decisions.

## Table of Contents

- [Overview](#overview)
- [Project Modules](#project-modules)
- [Project Structure](#project-structure)
- [Plugin System](#plugin-system)
- [Database Layer](#database-layer)
- [UI Components](#plugin.swisskit.hpl.ui-components)
- [Background Processing](#background-processing)
- [Excel Processing](#excel-processing)
- [Technology Stack](#technology-stack)

## Overview

SwissKit follows these architectural principles:

- **Modularity** - Each tool is a self-contained module
- **Extensibility** - Easy to add new tools via plugins
- **Performance** - Efficient processing with streaming and async operations
- **Maintainability** - Clean code structure and separation of concerns
- **Reusability** - Shared components in SwissKitJ-Api module

## Project Modules

SwissKit uses a multi-module Maven structure:

### SwissKitJ-Api Module

The API module provides shared components for both the core application and external plugins:

```
SwissKitJ-Api/
├── src/main/java/fan/summer/
│   ├── annoattion/
│   │   └── SwissKitPage.java        # Page annotation
│   ├── api/
│   │   └── KitPage.java             # Plugin interface
│   └── plugin.swisskit.hpl.ui/components/
│       ├── GradientProgressBar.java # Custom progress bar
│       └── FixedWidthComboBox.java  # Fixed-width combo box
└── pom.xml
```

**Key Features:**

- Generates source JAR and Javadoc JAR during package phase
- Only depends on Lombok (minimal dependencies)
- Must be installed to local Maven repository before building the main module

**Dependency for Plugins:**

```xml
<dependency>
    <groupId>fan.summer.api</groupId>
    <artifactId>SwissKitJ-Api</artifactId>
    <version>2.0.1</version>
</dependency>
```

### Main Module (SwissKit)

The core application with Excel, Email, and Settings tools.

### SwissKitJ-Plugin-Qcc

Example plugin project demonstrating plugin development.

### SwissKitJ-Plugin-HappyLearning

Auto-learning plugin demonstrating isolated plugin classloading with Java 11 HttpClient and fastjson2.

## Project Structure

```
SwissKit/

content.content:
1 # API Reference 2 3 This section provides detailed API documentation for SwissKit's core interfaces and classes. 4 5 ## Table of Contents 6 7 - [Module Overview](#module-overview) 8 - [KitPage Interface](#kitpage-interface) 9 - [@SwissKitPage Annotation](#swisskitpage-annotation) 10 - [SwissKitPageScanner](#swisskitpagescanner) 11 - [Database Layer](#database-layer) 12 - [UI Components](#plugin.swisskit.hpl.ui-components) 13 - [Worker Classes](#plugin.swisskit.hpl.worker-classes) 14 - [Utility Classes](#utility-classes) 15 - [Listeners](#listeners) 16 - [Entity Classes](#entity-classes) 17 18 ## Module Overview 19 20 SwissKit uses a multi-module architecture. Key components are organized as follows: 21 22 | Module | Package | Description | 23 |-------------------|------------------------------------------------|----------------------------| 24 | `SwissKitJ-Api` | `fan.summer.api` | Core interfaces (KitPage) | 25 | `SwissKitJ-Api` | `fan.summer.annoattion` | Annotations (SwissKitPage) | 26 | `SwissKitJ-Api` | `fan.summer.plugin.swisskit.hpl.ui.components` | Shared UI components | 27 | `SwissKit` (main) | `fan.summer.database` | Database layer | 28 | `SwissKit` (main) | `fan.summer.kitpage` | Tool implementations | 29 | `SwissKit` (main) | `fan.summer.utils` | Utility classes | 30 31 --- 32 33 ## KitPage Interface 34 35 The `KitPage` interface is the foundation for all tool pages in SwissKit. It is located in the `SwissKitJ-Api` module. 36 37 ### Location 38 39 ``` 40 SwissKitJ-Api/src/main/java/fan/summer/api/KitPage.java 41 ``` 42 43 ### Interface Definition 44 45 ```java 46 package fan.summer.api; 47 48 import fan.summer.annoattion.SwissKitPage; 49 50 import javax.swing.*; 51 52 /** 53 * Interface for SwissKit plugin pages. 54 * All plugin pages must implement this interface and be annotated with {@link SwissKitPage}. 55 */ 56 public interface KitPage { 57 /** 58 * Returns the main panel for this page. 59 * This panel will be displayed in the content area when the page is selected. 60 * 61 * @return the JPanel containing the page content 62 */ 63 JPanel getPanel(); 64 65 /** 66 * Returns the menu display name. 67 * Default implementation reads from {@link SwissKitPage#menuName()} annotation. 68 * 69 * @return menu display name 70 */ 71 default String getMenuName() { 72 SwissKitPage annotation = getClass().getAnnotation(SwissKitPage.class); 73 if (annotation != null && !annotation.menuName().isEmpty()) { 74 return annotation.menuName(); 75 } 76 return getClass().getSimpleName(); 77 } 78 79 /** 80 * Returns the menu icon. 81 * Override this method to provide a custom icon for the menu item. 82 * 83 * @return menu icon, or null for no icon 84 */ 85 default Icon getMenuIcon() { 86 return null; 87 } 88 89 /** 90 * Returns the menu tooltip text shown on hover. 91 * Default implementation reads from {@link SwissKitPage#menuTooltip()} annotation. 92 * 93 * @return tooltip text, or null for no tooltip 94 */ 95 default String getMenuTooltip() { 96 SwissKitPage annotation = getClass().getAnnotation(SwissKitPage.class); 97 if (annotation != null) { 98 return annotation.menuTooltip(); 99 } 100 return null; 101 } 102 } 103 ``` 104 105 ### Methods 106 107 | Method | Return Type | Description | 108 |--------------------|-------------|----------------------------------------------------------| 109 | `getPanel()` | `JPanel` | Returns the main panel containing all UI elements | 110 | `getMenuName()` | `String` | Returns the menu display name (default: from annotation) | 111 | `getMenuIcon()` | `Icon` | Returns the menu icon (default: `null`) | 112 | `getMenuTooltip()` | `String` | Returns the menu tooltip (default: from annotation) | 113 114 ### Example Implementation 115 116 ```java 117 import fan.summer.api.KitPage; 118 import fan.summer.annoattion.SwissKitPage; 119 120 @SwissKitPage( 121 menuName = "📋 Example", 122 menuTooltip = "Open Example Tool", 123 visible = true, 124 order = 10 125 ) 126 public class ExamplePage implements KitPage { 127 private JPanel panel; 128 129 public ExamplePage() { 130 panel = new JPanel(); 131 panel.add(new JLabel("Example Page")); 132 } 133 134 @Override 135 public JPanel getPanel() { 136 return panel; 137 } 138 } 139 ``` 140 141 --- 142 143 ## @SwissKitPage Annotation 144 145 The `@SwissKitPage` annotation configures tool page menu properties. It is located in the `SwissKitJ-Api` module. 146 147 ### Location 148 149 ``` 150 SwissKitJ-Api/src/main/java/fan/summer/annoattion/SwissKitPage.java 151 ``` 152 153 ### Annotation Definition 154 155 ```java 156 package fan.summer.annoattion; 157 158 import java.lang.annotation.*; 159 160 /** 161 * Annotation to mark a class as a KitPage implementation. 162 * Used for automatic page registration and menu configuration. 163 */ 164 @Retention(RetentionPolicy.RUNTIME) 165 @Target(ElementType.TYPE) 166 public @interface SwissKitPage { 167 /** 168 * Menu display name shown in the sidebar. 169 */ 170 String menuName() default ""; 171 172 /** 173 * Menu tooltip text shown on hover. 174 */ 175 String menuTooltip() default ""; 176 177 /** 178 * Whether this page is visible in the menu. 179 */ 180 boolean visible() default true; 181 182 /** 183 * Menu item order for sorting. 184 * Lower values appear first in the menu. 185 */ 186 int order() default 0; 187 } 188 ``` 189 190 ### Properties 191 192 | Property | Type | Default | Description | 193 |---------------|---------|---------|-------------------------------| 194 | `menuName` | String | "" | Display name in sidebar | 195 | `menuTooltip` | String | "" | Tooltip on hover | 196 | `visible` | boolean | true | Whether to show in menu | 197 | `order` | int | 0 | Display order (lower = first) | 198 199 --- 200 201 ## SwissKitPageScanner 202 203 The `SwissKitPageScaner` class automatically discovers and loads all `KitPage` implementations. 204 205 ### Location 206 207 ``` 208 src/main/java/fan/summer/scaner/SwissKitPageScaner.java 209 ``` 210 211 ```java 212 package fan.summer.scaner; 213 214 public class SwissKitPageScaner { 215 /** 216 * Scans for KitPage implementations using SPI ServiceLoader. 217 * 218 * @return list of visible KitPage instances sorted by order 219 */ 220 public static List scan() { 221 // Implementation 222 } 223 } 224 ``` 225 226 ### Key Features 227 228 - **SPI-based**: Uses Java ServiceLoader mechanism 229 - **Visibility Filter**: Only includes pages with `visible = true` 230 - **Order Sorting**: Sorts by `order()` value in annotation 231 - **Auto-Instantiation**: Creates page instances automatically 232 233 --- 234 235 ## Database Layer 236 237 ### DatabaseInit 238 239 Handles database initialization and MyBatis configuration. 240 241 ```java 242 package fan.summer.database; 243 244 public class DatabaseInit { 245 private static SqlSessionFactory sqlSessionFactory; 246 247 /** 248 * Initializes database and MyBatis. 249 * Creates tables from init.sql if not exist. 250 */ 251 public static void init() { } 252 253 /** 254 * Returns a new SqlSession for database operations. 255 */ 256 public static SqlSession getSqlSession() { } 257 258 /** 259 * Returns the SqlSessionFactory. 260 */ 261 public static SqlSessionFactory getSqlSessionFactory() { } 262 } 263 ``` 264 265 ### Entity Classes 266 267 #### EmailAddressBookEntity 268 269 ```java 270 @Data 271 public class EmailAddressBookEntity { 272 private Integer id; 273 private String emailAddress; 274 private String nickname; 275 private String tags; // JSON array 276 } 277 ``` 278 279 #### EmailTagEntity 280 281 ```java 282 @Data 283 public class EmailTagEntity { 284 private Long id; 285 private String tag; 286 } 287 ``` 288 289 #### EmailSentLogEntity 290 291 ```java 292 @Data 293 public class EmailSentLogEntity { 294 private Long id; 295 private String to; 296 private String cc; 297 private String bcc; 298 private String subject; 299 private String content; 300 private String attachment; 301 private Date sendTime; 302 private boolean isSuccess; 303 } 304 ``` 305 306 #### ComplexSplitConfigEntity 307 308 ```java 309 @Data 310 public class ComplexSplitConfigEntity { 311 private Long id; 312 private String taskId; 313 private String fieldName; 314 private String sheetName; 315 private Integer headerIndex; 316 private Integer columnIndex; 317 } 318 ``` 319 320 ### Mapper Interfaces 321 322 #### EmailAddressBookMapper 323 324 ```java 325 public interface EmailAddressBookMapper { 326 void insert(EmailAddressBookEntity entity); 327 List selectEmailAddressBook(); 328 } 329 ``` 330 331 #### EmailTagMapper 332 333 ```java 334 public interface EmailTagMapper { 335 void insert(EmailTagEntity entity); 336 void update(EmailTagEntity entity); 337 List selectAll(); 338 } 339 ``` 340 341 #### EmailSentLogMapper 342 343 ```java 344 public interface EmailSentLogMapper { 345 void insert(EmailSentLogEntity entity); 346 List selectAll(); 347 } 348 ``` 349 350 --- 351 352 ## UI Components 353 354 UI components are located in the `SwissKitJ-Api` module for shared use across the main application and plugins. 355 356 ### GradientProgressBar 357 358 A progress bar with gradient colors and smooth animation. 359 360 **Location**: `SwissKitJ-Api/src/main/java/fan/summer/plugin.swisskit.hpl.ui/components/GradientProgressBar.java` 361 362 #### Constructor 363 364 ```java ## [3.0.0-alpha.1] — First JavaFX Preview

**Release Date:** 2026-05-19

### ✨ Highlights

The entire application has been migrated from Swing/FlatLaf to **JavaFX 21**,
with a new glassmorphism dark theme, a redesigned plugin API, and a rebuilt
Excel Splitter with a multi-step wizard UI.

### ✨ New Features

- **JavaFX UI**: Complete migration from Swing/FlatLaf to JavaFX 21 with custom window chrome (`StageStyle.TRANSPARENT`), glassmorphism sidebar, glow-effect ToolCards, and animated step wizard
- **Plugin API v3**: New `SwissKitJPlugin` interface replaces the Swing-based `KitPage`; plugins return JavaFX `Node` instead of `JPanel`
- **Plugin Logger**: `fan.summer.api.log.LoggerFactory` with SLF4J/Logback backbone; plugin log calls are safe to call even before the host installs a binder
- **StepWizard**: Reusable multi-step wizard component in `SwissKitJ-Api` with dot navigation, slide transitions, step validation, and programmatic navigation
- **Plugin Store**: Online plugin catalog + local JAR install with hot-reload via `PluginLoader` file watcher
- **Three-Layer CSS Architecture**: `swisskit-common.css` (shared variables + glass utilities), `shell.css` (app chrome), `builtin.css` (tool styling) with automatic scene-graph inheritance
- **Type / Category / IconStyle Enums**: Type-safe enums replace String-based plugin metadata in the API module
- **Excel Splitter Wizard**: Redesigned with 4-step `StepWizard` flow: file select → analysis → split config → output

### 🔧 Fixes

- **Cross-Platform Native Libraries**: Fat JAR now bundles JavaFX native `.dll` (Windows), `.so` (Linux), and `.dylib` (macOS) so the same JAR runs on all three platforms regardless of build host

### ♻️ Refactors

- **Dependency Management**: All version properties centralized in parent POM (`javafx.version`, `lombok.version`, etc.)
- **Module Structure**: `SwissKitJ-Api` for shared API/UI, `SwissKit` for the app shell, `OfficalPlugin/` for bundled plugins
- **Backup Consolidation**: Legacy Swing sources moved to `backup/SwissKit/` and excluded from Maven compilation

### 📝 Documentation

- **README**: Complete rewrite for v3.0.0 JavaFX including build instructions, architecture overview, plugin development guide, and UI component reference

---

## 1.1.0

**Release Date:** 2026-03-30

# Changelog

All notable changes to SwissKit will be documented in this file.

The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.0.0/),
and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).

---

## [Unreleased]

### 🐛 Bug Fixes

- **Plugin Loading**: Handle plugin loading errors gracefully to ensure app startup when corrupted plugins exist

---

## 1.1.0

**Release Date:** 2026-03-30

---

### ✨ New Features

- **Plugin Hot-Deployment**: Deploy, reload, and uninstall plugins without restarting the application
- **HappyLearning Enhancement**: Add class hours tracking and status display

### 🔄 Changes

- **Project Restructuring**: Renamed `Happy-learning` → `SwissKitJ-Plugin-HappyLearning`, moved plugins to `OfficalPlugin/` directory
- **Module Organization**: Added `SwissKitJ-Api` as a shared API module

---

## 1.0.0

**Release Date:** 2026-03-29

---

### Introduction

SwissKit is a modular desktop productivity toolkit built with Java Swing, designed to simplify daily office tasks. It provides powerful Excel processing capabilities, email management with mass-sending features, and a flexible plugin system for extensibility.

**Key Characteristics:**
- Cross-platform: Windows, Linux, macOS (Apple Silicon)
- Plugin Architecture: Auto-discovers and loads plugins via Java SPI
- Modern UI: FlatLaf Look and Feel
- Database: H2 embedded database with MyBatis ORM
- Requirements: Java 11 or higher

---

### Features

#### Excel Kit
- **Complex Split Mode**: Split Excel files with complex configurations
- **Configuration Editor**: Visual editor for split parameters
- **Progress Tracking**: Real-time progress bar with animation

#### Email Kit
- **Mass Email Sending**: Send emails to recipients based on tags
- **Address Book**: Manage contacts with tag associations
- **Tag Management**: Organize recipients by tags
- **Sent Log**: Track email sending history with status

#### Plugin System
- **Auto-Discovery**: Automatically finds plugins in `META-INF/services`
- **Isolated Loading**: Plugins loaded in isolated ClassLoader
- **Easy Extension**: Implement `KitPage` interface to add new tools

#### Settings
- **Unified Configuration**: Centralized settings management
- **Plugin Management**: View and manage installed plugins

---

### Installation

#### System Requirements
- Java 11 or higher (JDK/JRE)
- Windows 10+, Linux (glibc 2.17+), or macOS 11+

#### Windows Installation
1. Download `SwissKit-windows.zip`
2. Extract to desired location
3. Run `SwissKit.exe`

#### Linux Installation
1. Download `SwissKit-linux.zip`
2. Extract: `unzip SwissKit-linux.zip`
3. Run: `./run.sh`

#### macOS Installation
1. Download `SwissKit-macos-apple-silicon.zip`
2. Extract: `unzip SwissKit-macos-apple-silicon.zip`
3. Run: `open SwissKit.app` or `./run.sh`

**Note:** Java 11+ is required. Download from [Adoptium](https://adoptium.net/).

---

### For Developers

#### Build from Source

```bash
# Install API module to local Maven repo (required)
mvn install -f SwissKitJ-Api/pom.xml -DskipTests

# Compile
mvn clean compile

# Package executable JAR
mvn clean package -DskipTests

# Run (development)
mvn exec:java -Dexec.mainClass="fan.summer.Main"
```

#### Project Structure
```
SwissKitJ/
├── SwissKitJ-Api/                         # Shared API module (interfaces, annotations)
├── SwissKit/                              # Main application
├── OfficalPlugin/
│   ├── SwissKitJ-Plugin-HappyLearning/   # Auto-learning plugin
│   └── SwissKitJ-Plugin-Qcc/              # CSV to Excel conversion plugin
└── docs/                                  # Documentation
```

#### Plugin Development
1. Create class implementing `KitPage` interface
2. Annotate with `@SwissKitPage`
3. Register in `META-INF/services/fan.summer.api.KitPage`
4. Tool appears automatically in sidebar

#### API Dependency
```xml
<dependency>
    <groupId>fan.summer.api</groupId>
    <artifactId>SwissKitJ-Api</artifactId>
    <version>1.0.0</version>
</dependency>
```

---

### Changelog

The following is the complete version history.

---

## 1.0.0-RC.1

### 🔧 Fixes

- ExcelSplitWorker: Fix EDT violation by using GradientProgressBar type instead of JProgressBar for proper animation
- EmailSentWorker: Division by zero guard when totalEmails is zero
- EmailSentWorker: NPE guard when tagCollect returns null
- SetComplexSplitConfigWorker: EDT violation fix - read Swing component values in constructor
- MassSentConfigView: NPE guard for config.getToTag()
- EmailKitPage: NPE guard for config.getToTag()/getCcTag() in equals comparison
- QueryAllEmailInfoWorker: NPE guard when JSON.parseArray returns null
- ConfigEditorView: Add null-safe objToString() helper for table values
- PluginLoader: Check mkdirs() return value
- GradientProgressBar: Stop animation timer in removeNotify() to prevent resource leaks
- ShowConfigViewWorker: Re-throw exceptions instead of silent catch

### 🔄 Changes

- Maven: Disable resource filtering to prevent binary icon files from being corrupted

---

## 1.0.0-Beta.4

### 🚀 Enhancements

- Settings: Add plugin management UI with installed plugins table and uninstall functionality
- HappyLearning: Display current lesson ID and name (brief) during learning sessions
- HappyLearning: Add progress bar text display showing "current/max h" format

### 🔧 Fixes

- EDT violations: SetComplexSplitConfigWorker and ClearComplexSplitConfigWorker now properly wrap UI updates in SwingUtilities.invokeLater()
- Resource leaks: EmailSentWorker SqlSession usage restructured with try-with-resources
- Resource leaks: ExcelUtil Workbook leaks fixed on exception paths
- NullPointerExceptions: Fixed in EmailSentWorker (progressBar, emailTags), EmailKitPage (tagList), EmailTagsView (parseLong), Main.java (getCause())
- Silent failures: QueryAllEmailInfoWorker now returns null instead of empty list on error for proper error handling
- Cancellation support: EmailSentWorker mass-sending loop now checks isCancelled()

---

## 1.0.0-Beta.3

### 🚀 Enhancements

- Email Address Book: Add double-click editing functionality for contacts
- Email Address Book: Reset button now clears only tag information

---

## 1.0.0-Beta.2

### 🔧 Fixes

- PluginLoader: IsolatedPluginClassLoader now properly delegates JDK classes (java.*, javax.*, sun.*, com.sun.*) to main
  app ClassLoader
- PluginLoader: Universal classloading fallback ensures plugin DTOs and third-party library classes are accessible from
  isolated plugin JARs

### 📝 Documentation

- Add IsolatedPluginClassLoader classloading strategy to architecture.md
- Fix JAR filename typos: Bata.1.jar → Beta.2.jar in documentation
- Update table alignment and spacing in README.md and docs/

---

## 1.0.0-Beta.1

### 🚀 Enhancements

- Add error dialog display when email sending fails

### 🔧 Fixes

- Center email sent log table layout in viewing dialog

---

## 1.0.0-Alpha5

### 🚀 Enhancements

- Add SwissKitJ-Api module for shared components and plugin development
- Add email sent log viewing functionality with status tracking

### 🔄 Changes

- Move KitPage interface to SwissKitJ-Api module
- Move SwissKitPage annotation to SwissKitJ-Api module
- Move UI components (GradientProgressBar, FixedWidthComboBox) to SwissKitJ-Api module

---

## 1.0-Alpha4

### 🚀 Enhancements

- Add progress bar support for email sending
- Display tag names instead of IDs in configuration view
- Improve progress bar real-time updates

---

## 1.0-Alpha3

### 🔄 Changes

- Refactor tag association mechanism to use tag ID instead of tag name

---

## 1.0-Alpha2

### 🚀 Enhancements

- Add email sending functionality with SMTP support
- Add mass email sending feature with tag-based recipients
- Add attachment support by tag-based folder selection

---

## 1.0-Alpha

### 🚀 Enhancements

- Add Excel complex split mode with custom configuration
- Add email address book management feature
- Add email tag management feature
- Add plugin loading system
- Add custom UI components (GradientProgressBar, FixedWidthComboBox)

+- **Plugin Logger**: `fan.summer.api.log.LoggerFactory` with SLF4J/Logback backbone; plugin log calls are safe to call even before the host installs a binder
+- **StepWizard**: Reusable multi-step wizard component in `SwissKitJ-Api` with dot navigation, slide transitions, step validation, and programmatic navigation
+- **Plugin Store**: Online plugin catalog + local JAR install with hot-reload via `PluginLoader` file watcher
+- **Three-Layer CSS Architecture**: `swisskit-common.css` (shared variables + glass utilities), `shell.css` (app chrome), `builtin.css` (tool styling) with automatic scene-graph inheritance
+- **Type / Category / IconStyle Enums**: Type-safe enums replace String-based plugin metadata in the API module
+- **Excel Splitter Wizard**: Redesigned with 4-step `StepWizard` flow: file select → analysis → split config → output
+- **Cross-Platform Native Libraries**: Fat JAR now bundles JavaFX native `.dll` (Windows), `.so` (Linux), and `.dylib` (macOS) so the same JAR runs on all three platforms regardless of build host
+- **Dependency Management**: All version properties centralized in parent POM (`javafx.version`, `lombok.version`, etc.)
+- **Module Structure**: `SwissKitJ-Api` for shared API/UI, `SwissKit` for the app shell, `OfficalPlugin/` for bundled plugins
+- **Backup Consolidation**: Legacy Swing sources moved to `backup/SwissKit/` and excluded from Maven compilation
+- **README**: Complete rewrite for v3.0.0 JavaFX including build instructions, architecture overview, plugin development guide, and UI component reference

- Maven 3.6+

- Build requires JDK 21, entry point is `fan.summer.Launcher`

- Category enum values: `TEXT`, `IMAGE`, `DEV`, `NET`, `OTHER`
- IconStyle enum values: `IC_BLUE`, `IC_PURPLE`, `IC_TEAL`, `IC_AMBER`, `IC_RED`, `IC_PINK`, `IC_GRAY`

content.content:
1	# Contributing
2	
3	Thank you for your interest in contributing to SwissKit! This document provides guidelines and instructions for contributing to the project.
4	
5	## Table of Contents
6	
7	- [Code of Conduct](#code-of-conduct)
8	- [Getting Started](#getting-started)
9	- [How to Contribute](#how-to-contribute)
10	- [Development Workflow](#development-workflow)
11	- [Pull Request Process](#pull-request-process)
12	- [Coding Standards](#coding-standards)
13	- [Reporting Issues](#reporting-issues)
14	
15	## Code of Conduct
16	
17	- Be respectful and inclusive
18	- Provide constructive feedback
19	- Welcome new contributors
20	- Focus on what is best for the community
21	
22	## Getting Started
23	
24	### Prerequisites
25	
26	- JDK 11 or higher
27	- Maven 3.6 or higher
28	- Git
29	- IntelliJ IDEA (recommended)
30	
31	### Setup
32	
33	```bash
34	# Fork the repository
35	# Clone your fork
36	git clone https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/SwissKitJ.git
37	cd SwissKit
38	
39	# Add upstream remote
40	git remote add upstream https://github.com/MuskStark/SwissKitJ.git
41	
42	# Build the project
43	mvn clean compile
44	
45	# Run the application
46	mvn exec:java -Dexec.mainClass="fan.summer.Main"
47	```
48	
49	## How to Contribute
50	
51	There are many ways to contribute:
52	
53	### Reporting Bugs
54	
55	1. Check existing [Issues](https://github.com/MuskStark/SwissKitJ/issues)
56	2. Create a new issue with:
57	   - Clear title
58	   - Detailed description
59	   - Steps to reproduce
60	   - Expected behavior
61	   - Actual behavior
62	   - Environment details (OS, Java version)
63	
64	### Suggesting Features
65	
66	1. Check existing feature requests
67	2. Create a new issue with:
68	   - Clear description of the feature
69	   - Use cases and benefits
70	   - Possible implementation approach
71	
72	### Submitting Code
73	
74	1. Fork the repository
75	2. Create a feature branch
76	3. Make your changes
77	4. Test thoroughly
78	5. Submit a pull request
79	
80	## Development Workflow
81	
82	### 1. Create a Branch
83	
84	```bash
85	git checkout -b feature/your-feature-name
86	```
87	
88	Branch naming conventions:
89	- `feature/` - New features
90	- `bugfix/` - Bug fixes
91	- `docs/` - Documentation updates
92	- `refactor/` - Code refactoring
93	
94	### 2. Make Changes
95	
96	- Write clean, readable code
97	- Add comments where necessary
98	- Update documentation
99	- Follow coding standards
100	
101	### 3. Test Your Changes
102	
103	```bash
104	# Compile
105	mvn clean compile
106	
107	# Run tests
108	mvn test
109	
110	# Build
111	mvn clean package
112	
113	# Run application
114	mvn exec:java -Dexec.mainClass="fan.summer.Main"
115	```
116	
117	### 4. Commit Your Changes
118	
119	```bash
120	git add .
121	git commit -m "type: description"
122	```
123	
124	Commit message format:
125	- `:sparkles:` - New feature
126	- `:art:` - Improve structure/format
127	- `:memo:` - Documentation
128	- `:bug:` - Bug fix
129	- `:arrow_up:` - Upgrade dependency
130	- `:recycle:` - Refactor code
131	
132	Examples:
133	```bash
134	git commit -m ":sparkles: Add PDF processing tool"
135	git commit -m ":bug: Fix progress bar not updating"
136	git commit -m ":memo: Update API documentation"
137	```
138	
139	### 5. Push to Your Fork
140	
141	```bash
142	git push origin feature/your-feature-name
143	```
144	
145	### 6. Create Pull Request
146	
147	1. Go to the original repository on GitHub
148	2. Click "New Pull Request"
149	3. Select your branch
150	4. Fill in the PR template
151	5. Submit
152	
153	## Pull Request Process
154	
155	### PR Template
156	
157	```markdown
158	## Description
159	Brief description of changes
160	
161	## Type of Change
162	- [ ] Bug fix
163	- [ ] New feature
164	- [ ] Breaking change
165	- [ ] Documentation update
166	
167	## Testing
168	Describe how you tested your changes
169	
170	## Checklist
171	- [ ] Code follows project style
172	- [ ] Self-reviewed the code
173	- [ ] Commented complex code
174	- [ ] Updated documentation
175	- [ ] No new warnings
176	- [ ] Added tests
177	- [ ] All tests pass
178	```
179	
180	### Review Process
181	
182	1. Automated checks run (CI)
183	2. Maintainer reviews your PR
184	3. Address any feedback
185	4. PR is merged
186	
187	## Coding Standards
188	
189	### Language
190	
191	- All code comments in English
192	- All UI text in English
193	- Variable and method names in English
194	
195	### Code Style
196	
197	- Follow existing code conventions
198	- Use meaningful names
199	- Add Javadoc for public APIs
200	- Keep methods focused and small
201	
202	### UI Standards
203	
204	- Use `SansSerif` font
205	- Use color constants from `UIUtils`
206	- Ensure consistent layout
207	
208	### Thread Safety
209	
210	- Update UI components in EDT thread only
211	- Use `SwingUtilities.invokeLater()` for UI updates
212	
213	```java
214	// Correct
215	SwingUtilities.invokeLater(() -> progressBar.setValue(50));
216	
217	// Incorrect
218	progressBar.setValue(50); // From background thread
219	```
220	
221	### Error Handling
222	
223	- Handle exceptions gracefully
224	- Provide user-friendly error messages
225	- Log errors for debugging
226	
227	```java
228	try {
229	    // Operation
230	} catch (Exception e) {
231	    logger.error("Operation failed", e);
232	    JOptionPane.showMessageDialog(parent,
233	            "Operation failed: " + e.getMessage(),
234	            "Error", JOptionPane.ERROR_MESSAGE);
235	}
236	```
237	
238	## Reporting Issues
239	
240	### Bug Report Template
241	
242	```markdown
243	## Description
244	Clear description of the bug
245	
246	## Steps to Reproduce
247	1. Step one
248	2. Step two
249	3. Step three
250	
251	## Expected Behavior
252	What should happen
253	
254	## Actual Behavior
255	What actually happens
256	
257	## Environment
258	- OS: [e.g., Windows 10, macOS 12]
259	- Java Version: [e.g., 11.0.15]
260	- SwissKit Version: [e.g., 1.0-SNAPSHOT]
261	
262	## Screenshots
263	If applicable, add screenshots
264	
265	## Additional Context
266	Any other relevant information
267	```
268	
269	### Feature Request Template
270	
271	```markdown
272	## Feature Description
273	Clear description of the feature
274	
275	## Use Cases
276	Describe the use cases
277	
278	## Proposed Solution
279	How you envision this feature working
280	
281	## Alternatives Considered
282	Other approaches you considered
283	
284	## Additional Context
285	Any other relevant information
286	```
287	
288	## Getting Help
289	
290	If you need help:
291	
292	1. Check existing documentation
293	2. Search existing issues
294	3. Ask in discussions or issues
295	
296	## Recognition
297	
298	Contributors will be acknowledged in the project's AUTHORS file.
299	
300	---
301	
302	**Thank you for contributing to SwissKit!** 🎉

# Contributing

Thank you for your interest in contributing to SwissKit! This document provides guidelines and instructions for contributing to the project.

## Table of Contents

- [Code of Conduct](#code-of-conduct)
- [Getting Started](#getting-started)
- [How to Contribute](#how-to-contribute)
- [Development Workflow](#development-workflow)
- [Pull Request Process](#pull-request-process)
- [Coding Standards](#coding-standards)
- [Reporting Issues](#reporting-issues)

## Code of Conduct

- Be respectful and inclusive
- Provide constructive feedback
- Welcome new contributors
- Focus on what is best for the community

## Getting Started

### Prerequisites

- JDK 11 or higher
- Maven 3.6 or higher
- Git
- IntelliJ IDEA (recommended)

### Setup

```bash
# Fork the repository
# Clone your fork
git clone https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/SwissKitJ.git
cd SwissKit

# Add upstream remote
git remote add upstream https://github.com/MuskStark/SwissKitJ.git

# Build the project
mvn clean compile

# Run the application
mvn exec:java -Dexec.mainClass="fan.summer.Main"
```

## How to Contribute

There are many ways to contribute:

### Reporting Bugs

1. Check existing [Issues](https://github.com/MuskStark/SwissKitJ/issues)
2. Create a new issue with:
   - Clear title
   - Detailed description
   - Steps to reproduce
   - Expected behavior
   - Actual behavior
   - Environment details (OS, Java version)

### Suggesting Features

1. Check existing feature requests
2. Create a new issue with:
   - Clear description of the feature
   - Use cases and benefits
   - Possible implementation approach

### Submitting Code

1. Fork the repository
2. Create a feature branch
3. Make your changes
4. Test thoroughly
5. Submit a pull request

## Development Workflow

### 1. Create a Branch

```bash
git checkout -b feature/your-feature-name
```

Branch naming conventions:
- `feature/` - New features
- `bugfix/` - Bug fixes
- `docs/` - Documentation updates
- `refactor/` - Code refactoring

### 2. Make Changes

- Write clean, readable code
- Add comments where necessary
- Update documentation
- Follow coding standards

### 3. Test Your Changes

```bash
# Compile
mvn clean compile

# Run tests
mvn test

# Build
mvn clean package

# Run application
mvn exec:java -Dexec.mainClass="fan.summer.Main"
```

### 4. Commit Your Changes

```bash
git add .
git commit -m "type: description"
```

Commit message format:
- `:sparkles:` - New feature
- `:art:` - Improve structure/format
- `:memo:` - Documentation
- `:bug:` - Bug fix
- `:arrow_up:` - Upgrade dependency
- `:recycle:` - Refactor code

Examples:
```bash
git commit -m ":sparkles: Add PDF processing tool"
git commit -m ":bug: Fix progress bar not updating"
git commit -m ":memo: Update API documentation"
```

### 5. Push to Your Fork

```bash
git push origin feature/your-feature-name
```

### 6. Create Pull Request

1. Go to the original repository on GitHub
2. Click "New Pull Request"
3. Select your branch
4. Fill in the PR template
5. Submit

## Pull Request Process

### PR Template

```markdown
## Description
Brief description of changes

## Type of Change
- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] New feature
- [ ] Breaking change
- [ ] Documentation update

## Testing
Describe how you tested your changes

## Checklist
- [ ] Code follows project style
- [ ] Self-reviewed the code
- [ ] Commented complex code
- [ ] Updated documentation
- [ ] No new warnings
- [ ] Added tests
- [ ] All tests pass
```

### Review Process

1. Automated checks run (CI)
2. Maintainer reviews your PR
3. Address any feedback
4. PR is merged

## Coding Standards

### Language

- All code comments in English
- All UI text in English
- Variable and method names in English

### Code Style

- Follow existing code conventions
- Use meaningful names
- Add Javadoc for public APIs
- Keep methods focused and small

### UI Standards

- Use `SansSerif` font
- Use color constants from `UIUtils`
- Ensure consistent layout

### Thread Safety

- Update UI components in EDT thread only
- Use `SwingUtilities.invokeLater()` for UI updates

```java
// Correct
SwingUtilities.invokeLater(() -> progressBar.setValue(50));

// Incorrect
progressBar.setValue(50); // From background thread
```

### Error Handling

- Handle exceptions gracefully
- Provide user-friendly error messages
- Log errors for debugging

```java
try {
    // Operation
} catch (Exception e) {
    logger.error("Operation failed", e);
    JOptionPane.showMessageDialog(parent,
            "Operation failed: " + e.getMessage(),
            "Error", JOptionPane.ERROR_MESSAGE);
}
```

## Reporting Issues

### Bug Report Template

```markdown
## Description
Clear description of the bug

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Step one
2. Step two
3. Step three

## Expected Behavior
What should happen

## Actual Behavior
What actually happens

## Environment
- OS: [e.g., Windows 10, macOS 12]
- Java Version: [e.g., 11.0.15]
- SwissKit Version: [e.g., 1.0-SNAPSHOT]

## Screenshots
If applicable, add screenshots

## Additional Context
Any other relevant information
```

### Feature Request Template

```markdown
## Feature Description
Clear description of the feature

## Use Cases
Describe the use cases

## Proposed Solution
How you envision this feature working

## Alternatives Considered
Other approaches you considered

## Additional Context
Any other relevant information
```

## Getting Help

If you need help:

1. Check existing documentation
2. Search existing issues
3. Ask in discussions or issues

## Recognition

Contributors will be acknowledged in the project's AUTHORS file.

---

**Thank you for contributing to SwissKit!** 🎉

input.content:
# Features

SwissKit provides a variety of built-in tools organized by category. You can also extend functionality with external plugins.

## Categories

The sidebar organizes tools into six categories:

| Category | Icon | Purpose |
|----------|------|---------|
| ALL | — | Show all tools |
| TEXT | `T` | Text processing |
| IMAGE | `I` | Image tools |
| DEV | `<>` | Developer utilities |
| NET | `@` | Network / communication |
| OTHER | `···` | Everything else |

## Built-in Tools

### Developer Tools (`DEV`)

#### JSON Formatter
Format, compress, and validate JSON data. Paste raw JSON and get pretty-printed output instantly.

#### Base64
Encode text to Base64 or decode Base64 strings back to plain text.

#### Hash Calculator
Compute MD5, SHA-1, SHA-256, and SHA-512 checksums for any text input.

---

### Text Tools (`TEXT`)

#### Markdown Editor
Real-time Markdown editor with side-by-side preview. Write in Markdown on the left, see rendered HTML on the right.

---

### Image Tools (`IMAGE`)

#### Color Converter
Convert between HEX, RGB, and HSL color formats with a live color preview swatch.

---

### Network Tools (`NET`)

#### Email
Send emails with SMTP configuration. Supports:
- Single and mass email sending by recipient tags
- Attachment routing by tag-based folder
- Address book with tag management
- Sent mail history log

---

### Other Tools (`OTHER`)

#### Excel Splitter
Split Excel files using a 4-step wizard:

1. **Select File** — Choose the source `.xlsx` or `.xls` file
2. **Analysis** — Auto-detects all sheets and their headers
3. **Split Mode** — Choose from three modes:
   - **Split by Sheet** — One output file per sheet
   - **Split by Column** — Group rows by unique column values
   - **Complex Split** — Multi-config split from database settings
4. **Output** — Choose output directory and start processing

Progress is shown in real-time with percentage updates.

---

## System Features

### Plugin Store
Browse and install plugins from the online catalog, or load a local plugin JAR. Installed plugins are hot-reloaded automatically.

### Settings
Configure SMTP email server settings, manage the email address book and tags, and view installed plugins.

---

## Plugin System

SwissKit discovers plugins in two ways:

- **Built-in tools** are registered directly by `BuiltinToolRegistrar` at startup.
- **External plugins** implement `SwissKitJPlugin` and declare it in `META-INF/services/`. Drop the JAR into the `plugins/` directory and it is loaded automatically with hot-reload support.

See the [Architecture](architecture.md) page for how plugins are loaded, and [Development](development.md) for building your own.

---

**Ready to build one?** Head to the [Development Guide](development.md).

input.content:
# Architecture

SwissKit is built with a modular, plugin-based architecture on JavaFX 21.

## Module Structure

| Module | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `SwissKitJ-Api` | Shared plugin interface (`SwissKitJPlugin`), reusable components (`StepWizard`), theming utilities, logging API |
| `SwissKit` | JavaFX application shell — UI, plugin loading, built-in tools |
| `OfficalPlugin/*` | Bundled plugins (HappyLearning, Qcc, Mouse) |

All plugins declare `SwissKitJ-Api` as `provided` scope. The main app provides it at runtime via the fat JAR.

## Startup Sequence

`fan.summer.Launcher` (fat-JAR manifest entry point) → `fan.summer.app.SwissKitJApp` (JavaFX `Application`).

In `SwissKitJApp.start()`:

1. Resolve `plugins/` directory (JAR sibling in production, `./plugins/` in dev)
2. Create `PluginLoader` + `PluginRegistry`
3. Register built-in tools via `BuiltinToolRegistrar` (7 tools across 5 categories)
4. Build `MainWindow` and display it
5. Start `PluginLoader` (scans `plugins/` dir and watches for file changes)

## UI Structure

All UI lives in `fan.summer.ui.*`:

| Component | Role |
|-----------|------|
| `MainWindow` | Root `StackPane`; owns `TitleBar`, `Sidebar`, `ContentArea`, status bar |
| `Sidebar` | Category-based navigation with search bar; categories: all / text / image / dev / net / other |
| `ContentArea` | Shows `ToolCard` grid or active tool view; manages `DetailPanel` and back-bar |
| `DetailPanel` | Slide-in panel showing plugin metadata with a Launch button |
| `TitleBar` | Custom window chrome (window is `StageStyle.TRANSPARENT`) |

### Navigation Flow

`ToolCard` click → `DetailPanel.show()` → Launch button → `registry.activate(plugin)` + `contentArea.showPage(plugin.createView(), title)`.

The back bar (shown by `ContentArea`) calls `registry.deactivate()` on return.

## Plugin System

### Interface (`SwissKitJPlugin`)

```java
public interface SwissKitJPlugin {
    String getId();          // reverse-domain ID, e.g. "com.example.my-tool"
    String getName();
    String getDescription();
    ToolCategory getCategory();    // TEXT / IMAGE / DEV / NET / OTHER
    String getVersion();
    String getIconText();    // emoji or single char
    default IconStyle getIconStyle() { return IconStyle.IC_BLUE; }
    default PluginType getType()    { return PluginType.PLUGIN; }

    Node createView();       // called once; result cached and reused
    default void onActivate()   {}
    default void onDeactivate() {}
    default void onUnload()     {}
}
```

### Registration

- **Built-in tools**: Registered directly by `BuiltinToolRegistrar` — no SPI needed.
- **External plugins**: Implement `SwissKitJPlugin`, declare in `META-INF/services/fan.summer.api.SwissKitJPlugin`, drop JAR into `plugins/`. Hot-reload via file watcher.

### Plugin Logging

Plugins use `fan.summer.api.log.LoggerFactory` which routes to SLF4J/Logback when the host app is running, and returns a silent no-op logger in tests.

## CSS Theming

Three-layer architecture (glassmorphism dark theme):

| File | Module | Scope |
|------|--------|-------|
| `css/swisskit-common.css` | `SwissKitJ-Api` | Shared variables, scrollbars, `.glass-*` utilities |
| `css/shell.css` | `SwissKit` | App chrome — titlebar, sidebar, tool cards, detail panel, status bar |
| `css/builtin.css` | `SwissKit` | Reserved for built-in tool styling |

Plugins embedded in the main Scene automatically inherit all three stylesheets via scene-graph propagation. Plugins opening their own `Stage`/`Scene` can call `Themes.applyTo(scene)` for the common utilities.

## Database

H2 embedded database at `.swisskit/swisskit.db` relative to the working directory. Schema initialized from `init.sql`. Data access via MyBatis with XML mappers in `src/main/resources/mapper/`.

## Threading

JavaFX uses a single UI thread (the JavaFX Application Thread). All UI updates must happen on this thread:

- Use `Platform.runLater()` for UI updates from background threads.
- Long-running work (file I/O, network, DB) should run on a background thread via `Task` or `ExecutorService`.

## Build

```bash
mvn install -f SwissKitJ-Api/pom.xml -DskipTests
mvn clean package -DskipTests
java -jar SwissKit/target/SwissKitJ-3.0.0-alpha.1.jar
```

The fat JAR is built by `maven-shade-plugin` and bundles JavaFX native libraries for all three platforms (Windows `.dll`, Linux `.so`, macOS `.dylib`).

|--------|---------|
| `SwissKitJ-Api` | Shared plugin interface (`SwissKitJPlugin`), reusable components (`StepWizard`), theming utilities, logging API |
| `SwissKit` | JavaFX application shell — UI, plugin loading, built-in tools |
| `OfficalPlugin/*` | Bundled plugins (HappyLearning, Qcc, Mouse) |

All plugins declare `SwissKitJ-Api` as `provided` scope. The main app provides it at runtime via the fat JAR.

## Startup Sequence

`fan.summer.Launcher` (fat-JAR manifest entry point) → `fan.summer.app.SwissKitJApp` (JavaFX `Application`).

In `SwissKitJApp.start()`:

1. Resolve `plugins/` directory (JAR sibling in production, `./plugins/` in dev)
2. Create `PluginLoader` + `PluginRegistry`
3. Register built-in tools via `BuiltinToolRegistrar` (7 tools across 5 categories)
4. Build `MainWindow` and display it
5. Start `PluginLoader` (scans `plugins/` dir and watches for file changes)

## UI Structure

All UI lives in `fan.summer.ui.*`:

| Component | Role |
|-----------|------|
| `MainWindow` | Root `StackPane`; owns `TitleBar`, `Sidebar`, `ContentArea`, status bar |
| `Sidebar` | Category-based navigation with search bar; categories: all / text / image / dev / net / other |
| `ContentArea` | Shows `ToolCard` grid or active tool view; manages `DetailPanel` and back-bar |
| `DetailPanel` | Slide-in panel showing plugin metadata with a Launch button |
| `TitleBar` | Custom window chrome (window is `StageStyle.TRANSPARENT`) |

### Navigation Flow

`ToolCard` click → `DetailPanel.show()` → Launch button → `registry.activate(plugin)` + `contentArea.showPage(plugin.createView(), title)`.

The back bar (shown by `ContentArea`) calls `registry.deactivate()` on return.

## Plugin System

### Interface (`SwissKitJPlugin`)

```java
public interface SwissKitJPlugin {
    String getId();          // reverse-domain ID, e.g. "com.example.my-tool"
    String getName();
    String getDescription();
    ToolCategory getCategory();    // TEXT / IMAGE / DEV / NET / OTHER
    String getVersion();
    String getIconText();    // emoji or single char
    default IconStyle getIconStyle() { return IconStyle.IC_BLUE; }
    default PluginType getType()    { return PluginType.PLUGIN; }

    Node createView();       // called once; result cached and reused
    default void onActivate()   {}
    default void onDeactivate() {}
    default void onUnload()     {}
}
```

### Registration

- **Built-in tools**: Registered directly by `BuiltinToolRegistrar` — no SPI needed.
- **External plugins**: Implement `SwissKitJPlugin`, declare in `META-INF/services/fan.summer.api.SwissKitJPlugin`, drop JAR into `plugins/`. Hot-reload via file watcher.

### Plugin Logging

Plugins use `fan.summer.api.log.LoggerFactory` which routes to SLF4J/Logback when the host app is running, and returns a silent no-op logger in tests.

## CSS Theming

Three-layer architecture (glassmorphism dark theme):

| File | Module | Scope |
|------|--------|-------|
| `css/swisskit-common.css` | `SwissKitJ-Api` | Shared variables, scrollbars, `.glass-*` utilities |
| `css/shell.css` | `SwissKit` | App chrome — titlebar, sidebar, tool cards, detail panel, status bar |
| `css/builtin.css` | `SwissKit` | Reserved for built-in tool styling |

Plugins embedded in the main Scene automatically inherit all three stylesheets via scene-graph propagation. Plugins opening their own `Stage`/`Scene` can call `Themes.applyTo(scene)` for the common utilities.

## Database

H2 embedded database at `.swisskit/swisskit.db` relative to the working directory. Schema initialized from `init.sql`. Data access via MyBatis with XML mappers in `src/main/resources/mapper/`.

## Threading

JavaFX uses a single UI thread (the JavaFX Application Thread). All UI updates must happen on this thread:

- Use `Platform.runLater()` for UI updates from background threads.
- Long-running work (file I/O, network, DB) should run on a background thread via `Task` or `ExecutorService`.

## Build

```bash
mvn install -f SwissKitJ-Api/pom.xml -DskipTests
mvn clean package -DskipTests
java -jar SwissKit/target/SwissKitJ-3.0.0-alpha.1.jar
```

The fat JAR is built by `maven-shade-plugin` and bundles JavaFX native libraries for all three platforms (Windows `.dll`, Linux `.so`, macOS `.dylib`).

input.content:
# Contributing

Thank you for your interest in contributing to SwissKit! This document provides guidelines and instructions for contributing to the project.

## Prerequisites

- JDK 21 or higher
- Maven 3.6 or higher
- Git
- IntelliJ IDEA (recommended)

## Setup

```bash
# Fork the repository, then clone your fork
git clone https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/SwissKitJ.git
cd SwissKitJ

# Add upstream remote
git remote add upstream https://github.com/MuskStark/SwissKitJ.git

# Install API module first (required)
mvn install -f SwissKitJ-Api/pom.xml -DskipTests

# Build the project
mvn clean compile -DskipTests

# Run the application
java -jar SwissKit/target/SwissKitJ-3.0.0-alpha.1.jar
```

## Development Workflow

### Branch naming

- `feature/` — New features
- `bugfix/` — Bug fixes
- `docs/` — Documentation updates
- `refactor/` — Code refactoring

### Commit message format

Use conventional commits with emojis:

| Prefix | Emoji | Purpose |
|--------|-------|---------|
| `✨ feat:` | `:sparkles:` | New feature |
| `🐛 fix:` | `:bug:` | Bug fix |
| `♻️ refactor:` | `:recycle:` | Refactoring |
| `📝 docs:` | `:memo:` | Documentation |
| `⬆️ deps:` | `:arrow_up:` | Dependency upgrade |

### Creating a PR

1. Fork the repository and create a feature branch
2. Make your changes, following the coding standards below
3. Build and test: `mvn clean package -DskipTests`
4. Commit with conventional commit format
5. Push to your fork and open a pull request against `main`

## Coding Standards

### Language

- All code comments in English
- All UI text in English
- Variable and method names in English

### Thread Safety

JavaFX has a single UI thread. All UI updates must happen on it:

```java
// Correct — update UI from background thread
Platform.runLater(() -> progressBar.setProgress(0.5));

// Also correct — update on JavaFX Application Thread directly
label.setText("Done");
```

### Error Handling

```java
import javafx.scene.control.Alert;
import javafx.scene.control.Alert.AlertType;

try {
    // Operation
} catch (Exception e) {
    log.error("Operation failed", e);
    Alert alert = new Alert(AlertType.ERROR, "Operation failed: " + e.getMessage());
    alert.showAndWait();
}
```

## Reporting Issues

Include:
- OS and version
- Java version (`java -version`)
- SwissKit version
- Steps to reproduce
- Expected vs actual behavior
- Screenshots if applicable

## Getting Help

1. Check the documentation in `docs/`
2. Search existing [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/MuskStark/SwissKitJ/issues)
3. Create a new issue with details

---

**Thank you for contributing to SwissKit!**

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Contributing

Thank you for your interest in contributing to SwissKit! This document provides guidelines and instructions for contributing to the project.

## Table of Contents

- [Code of Conduct](#code-of-conduct)
- [Getting Started](#getting-started)
- [How to Contribute](#how-to-contribute)
- [Development Workflow](#development-workflow)
- [Pull Request Process](#pull-request-process)
- [Coding Standards](#coding-standards)
- [Reporting Issues](#reporting-issues)

## Code of Conduct

- Be respectful and inclusive
- Provide constructive feedback
- Welcome new contributors
- Focus on what is best for the community

## Getting Started

### Prerequisites

- JDK 11 or higher
- Maven 3.6 or higher
- Git
- IntelliJ IDEA (recommended)

### Setup

```bash
# Fork the repository
# Clone your fork
git clone https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/SwissKitJ.git
cd SwissKit

# Add upstream remote
git remote add upstream https://github.com/MuskStark/SwissKitJ.git

# Build the project
mvn clean compile

# Run the application
mvn exec:java -Dexec.mainClass="fan.summer.Main"
```

## How to Contribute

There are many ways to contribute:

### Reporting Bugs

1. Check existing [Issues](https://github.com/MuskStark/SwissKitJ/issues)
2. Create a new issue with:
   - Clear title
   - Detailed description
   - Steps to reproduce
   - Expected behavior
   - Actual behavior
   - Environment details (OS, Java version)

### Suggesting Features

1. Check existing feature requests
2. Create a new issue with:
   - Clear description of the feature
   - Use cases and benefits
   - Possible implementation approach

### Submitting Code

1. Fork the repository
2. Create a feature branch
3. Make your changes
4. Test thoroughly
5. Submit a pull request

## Development Workflow

### 1. Create a Branch

```bash
git checkout -b feature/your-feature-name
```

Branch naming conventions:
- `feature/` - New features
- `bugfix/` - Bug fixes
- `docs/` - Documentation updates
- `refactor/` - Code refactoring

### 2. Make Changes

- Write clean, readable code
- Add comments where necessary
- Update documentation
- Follow coding standards

### 3. Test Your Changes

```bash
# Compile
mvn clean compile

# Run tests
mvn test

# Build
mvn clean package

# Run application
mvn exec:java -Dexec.mainClass="fan.summer.Main"
```

### 4. Commit Your Changes

```bash
git add .
git commit -m "type: description"
```

Commit message format:
- `:sparkles:` - New feature
- `:art:` - Improve structure/format
- `:memo:` - Documentation
- `:bug:` - Bug fix
- `:arrow_up:` - Upgrade dependency
- `:recycle:` - Refactor code

Examples:
```bash
git commit -m ":sparkles: Add PDF processing tool"
git commit -m ":bug: Fix progress bar not updating"
git commit -m ":memo: Update API documentation"
```

### 5. Push to Your Fork

```bash
git push origin feature/your-feature-name
```

### 6. Create Pull Request

1. Go to the original repository on GitHub
2. Click "New Pull Request"
3. Select your branch
4. Fill in the PR template
5. Submit

## Pull Request Process

### PR Template

```markdown
## Description
Brief description of changes

## Type of Change
- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] New feature
- [ ] Breaking change
- [ ] Documentation update

## Testing
Describe how you tested your changes

## Checklist
- [ ] Code follows project style
- [ ] Self-reviewed the code
- [ ] Commented complex code
- [ ] Updated documentation
- [ ] No new warnings
- [ ] Added tests
- [ ] All tests pass
```

### Review Process

1. Automated checks run (CI)
2. Maintainer reviews your PR
3. Address any feedback
4. PR is merged

## Coding Standards

### Language

- All code comments in English
- All UI text in English
- Variable and method names in English

### Code Style

- Follow existing code conventions
- Use meaningful names
- Add Javadoc for public APIs
- Keep methods focused and small

### UI Standards

- Use `SansSerif` font
- Use color constants from `UIUtils`
- Ensure consistent layout

### Thread Safety

- Update UI components in EDT thread only
- Use `SwingUtilities.invokeLater()` for UI updates

```java
// Correct
SwingUtilities.invokeLater(() -> progressBar.setValue(50));

// Incorrect
progressBar.setValue(50); // From background thread
```

### Error Handling

- Handle exceptions gracefully
- Provide user-friendly error messages
- Log errors for debugging

```java
try {
    // Operation
} catch (Exception e) {
    logger.error("Operation failed", e);
    JOptionPane.showMessageDialog(parent,
            "Operation failed: " + e.getMessage(),
            "Error", JOptionPane.ERROR_MESSAGE);
}
```

## Reporting Issues

### Bug Report Template

```markdown
## Description
Clear description of the bug

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Step one
2. Step two
3. Step three

## Expected Behavior
What should happen

## Actual Behavior
What actually happens

## Environment
- OS: [e.g., Windows 10, macOS 12]
- Java Version: [e.g., 11.0.15]
- SwissKit Version: [e.g., 1.0-SNAPSHOT]

## Screenshots
If applicable, add screenshots

## Additional Context
Any other relevant information
```

### Feature Request Template

```markdown
## Feature Description
Clear description of the feature

## Use Cases
Describe the use cases

## Proposed Solution
How you envision this feature working

## Alternatives Considered
Other approaches you considered

## Additional Context
Any other relevant information
```

## Getting Help

If you need help:

1. Check existing documentation
2. Search existing issues
3. Ask in discussions or issues

## Recognition

Contributors will be acknowledged in the project's AUTHORS file.

---

**Thank you for contributing to SwissKit!** 🎉

1	# Changelog
2	
3	All notable changes to SwissKit will be documented in this file.
4	
5	The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.0.0/),
6	and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).
7	
8	---
9	
10	## [3.0.0-alpha.1] — First JavaFX Preview
11	
12	**Release Date:** 2026-05-19
13	
14	### ✨ Highlights
15	
16	The entire application has been migrated from Swing/FlatLaf to **JavaFX 21**,
17	with a new glassmorphism dark theme, a redesigned plugin API, and a rebuilt
18	Excel Splitter with a multi-step wizard UI.
19	
20	### ✨ New Features

file.content:
# Changelog

All notable changes to SwissKit will be documented in this file.

The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.0.0/),
and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).

---

## [3.0.0-alpha.1] — First JavaFX Preview

**Release Date:** 2026-05-19

### ✨ Highlights

The entire application has been migrated from Swing/FlatLaf to **JavaFX 21**,
with a new glassmorphism dark theme, a redesigned plugin API, and a rebuilt
Excel Splitter with a multi-step wizard UI.

### ✨ New Features

- **JavaFX UI**: Complete migration from Swing/FlatLaf to JavaFX 21 with custom window chrome (`StageStyle.TRANSPARENT`), glassmorphism sidebar, glow-effect ToolCards, and animated step wizard
- **Plugin API v3**: New `SwissKitJPlugin` interface replaces the Swing-based `KitPage`; plugins return JavaFX `Node` instead of `JPanel`
- **Plugin Logger**: `fan.summer.api.log.LoggerFactory` with SLF4J/Logback backbone; plugin log calls are safe to call even before the host installs a binder
- **StepWizard**: Reusable multi-step wizard component in `SwissKitJ-Api` with dot navigation, slide transitions, step validation, and programmatic navigation
- **Plugin Store**: Online plugin catalog + local JAR install with hot-reload via `PluginLoader` file watcher
- **Three-Layer CSS Architecture**: `swisskit-common.css` (shared variables + glass utilities), `shell.css` (app chrome), `builtin.css` (tool styling) with automatic scene-graph inheritance
- **Type / Category / IconStyle Enums**: Type-safe enums replace String-based plugin metadata in the API module
- **Excel Splitter Wizard**: Redesigned with 4-step `StepWizard` flow: file select → analysis → split config → output

### 🔧 Fixes

- **Cross-Platform Native Libraries**: Fat JAR now bundles JavaFX native `.dll` (Windows), `.so` (Linux), and `.dylib` (macOS) so the same JAR runs on all three platforms regardless of build host

### ♻️ Refactors

- **Dependency Management**: All version properties centralized in parent POM (`javafx.version`, `lombok.version`, etc.)
- **Module Structure**: `SwissKitJ-Api` for shared API/UI, `SwissKit` for the app shell, `OfficalPlugin/` for bundled plugins
- **Backup Consolidation**: Legacy Swing sources moved to `backup/SwissKit/` and excluded from Maven compilation

### 📝 Documentation

- **README**: Complete rewrite for v3.0.0 JavaFX including build instructions, architecture overview, plugin development guide, and UI component reference

---

## 1.1.0

**Release Date:** 2026-03-30

---

### ✨ New Features

- **Plugin Hot-Deployment**: Deploy, reload, and uninstall plugins without restarting the application
- **HappyLearning Enhancement**: Add class hours tracking and status display

### 🔄 Changes

- **Project Restructuring**: Renamed `Happy-learning` → `SwissKitJ-Plugin-HappyLearning`, moved plugins to `OfficalPlugin/` directory
- **Module Organization**: Added `SwissKitJ-Api` as a shared API module

---

## 1.0.0

**Release Date:** 2026-03-29

---

### Introduction

SwissKit is a modular desktop productivity toolkit built with Java Swing, designed to simplify daily office tasks. It provides powerful Excel processing capabilities, email management with mass-sending features, and a flexible plugin system for extensibility.

**Key Characteristics:**
- Cross-platform: Windows, Linux, macOS (Apple Silicon)
- Plugin Architecture: Auto-discovers and loads plugins via Java SPI
- Modern UI: FlatLaf Look and Feel
- Database: H2 embedded database with MyBatis ORM
- Requirements: Java 11 or higher

---

### Features

#### Excel Kit
- **Complex Split Mode**: Split Excel files with complex configurations
- **Configuration Editor**: Visual editor for split parameters
- **Progress Tracking**: Real-time progress bar with animation

#### Email Kit
- **Mass Email Sending**: Send emails to recipients based on tags
- **Address Book**: Manage contacts with tag associations
- **Tag Management**: Organize recipients by tags
- **Sent Log**: Track email sending history with status

#### Plugin System
- **Auto-Discovery**: Automatically finds plugins in `META-INF/services`
- **Isolated Loading**: Plugins loaded in isolated ClassLoader
- **Easy Extension**: Implement `KitPage` interface to add new tools

#### Settings
- **Unified Configuration**: Centralized settings management
- **Plugin Management**: View and manage installed plugins

---

### Installation

#### System Requirements
- Java 11 or higher (JDK/JRE)
- Windows 10+, Linux (glibc 2.17+), or macOS 11+

#### Windows Installation
1. Download `SwissKit-windows.zip`
2. Extract to desired location
3. Run `SwissKit.exe`

#### Linux Installation
1. Download `SwissKit-linux.zip`
2. Extract: `unzip SwissKit-linux.zip`
3. Run: `./run.sh`

#### macOS Installation
1. Download `SwissKit-macos-apple-silicon.zip`
2. Extract: `unzip SwissKit-macos-apple-silicon.zip`
3. Run: `open SwissKit.app` or `./run.sh`

**Note:** Java 11+ is required. Download from [Adoptium](https://adoptium.net/).

---

### For Developers

#### Build from Source

```bash
# Install API module to local Maven repo (required)
mvn install -f SwissKitJ-Api/pom.xml -DskipTests

# Compile
mvn clean compile

# Package executable JAR
mvn clean package -DskipTests

# Run (development)
mvn exec:java -Dexec.mainClass="fan.summer.Main"
```

#### Project Structure
```
SwissKitJ/
├── SwissKitJ-Api/                         # Shared API module (interfaces, annotations)
├── SwissKit/                              # Main application
├── OfficalPlugin/
│   ├── SwissKitJ-Plugin-HappyLearning/   # Auto-learning plugin
│   └── SwissKitJ-Plugin-Qcc/              # CSV to Excel conversion plugin
└── docs/                                  # Documentation
```

#### Plugin Development
1. Create class implementing `KitPage` interface
2. Annotate with `@SwissKitPage`
3. Register in `META-INF/services/fan.summer.api.KitPage`
4. Tool appears automatically in sidebar

#### API Dependency
```xml
<dependency>
    <groupId>fan.summer.api</groupId>
    <artifactId>SwissKitJ-Api</artifactId>
    <version>1.0.0</version>
</dependency>
```

---

### Changelog

The following is the complete version history.

---

## 1.0.0-RC.1

### 🔧 Fixes

- ExcelSplitWorker: Fix EDT violation by using GradientProgressBar type instead of JProgressBar for proper animation
- EmailSentWorker: Division by zero guard when totalEmails is zero
- EmailSentWorker: NPE guard when tagCollect returns null
- SetComplexSplitConfigWorker: EDT violation fix - read Swing component values in constructor
- MassSentConfigView: NPE guard for config.getToTag()
- EmailKitPage: NPE guard for config.getToTag()/getCcTag() in equals comparison
- QueryAllEmailInfoWorker: NPE guard when JSON.parseArray returns null
- ConfigEditorView: Add null-safe objToString() helper for table values
- PluginLoader: Check mkdirs() return value
- GradientProgressBar: Stop animation timer in removeNotify() to prevent resource leaks
- ShowConfigViewWorker: Re-throw exceptions instead of silent catch

### 🔄 Changes

- Maven: Disable resource filtering to prevent binary icon files from being corrupted

---

## 1.0.0-Beta.4

### 🚀 Enhancements

- Settings: Add plugin management UI with installed plugins table and uninstall functionality
- HappyLearning: Display current lesson ID and name (brief) during learning sessions
- HappyLearning: Add progress bar text display showing "current/max h" format

### 🔧 Fixes

- EDT violations: SetComplexSplitConfigWorker and ClearComplexSplitConfigWorker now properly wrap UI updates in SwingUtilities.invokeLater()
- Resource leaks: EmailSentWorker SqlSession usage restructured with try-with-resources
- Resource leaks: ExcelUtil Workbook leaks fixed on exception paths
- NullPointerExceptions: Fixed in EmailSentWorker (progressBar, emailTags), EmailKitPage (tagList), EmailTagsView (parseLong), Main.java (getCause())
- Silent failures: QueryAllEmailInfoWorker now returns null instead of empty list on error for proper error handling
- Cancellation support: EmailSentWorker mass-sending loop now checks isCancelled()

---

## 1.0.0-Beta.3

### 🚀 Enhancements

- Email Address Book: Add double-click editing functionality for contacts
- Email Address Book: Reset button now clears only tag information

---

## 1.0.0-Beta.2

### 🔧 Fixes

- PluginLoader: IsolatedPluginClassLoader now properly delegates JDK classes (java.*, javax.*, sun.*, com.sun.*) to main
  app ClassLoader
- PluginLoader: Universal classloading fallback ensures plugin DTOs and third-party library classes are accessible from
  isolated plugin JARs

### 📝 Documentation

- Add IsolatedPluginClassLoader classloading strategy to architecture.md
- Fix JAR filename typos: Bata.1.jar → Beta.2.jar in documentation
- Update table alignment and spacing in README.md and docs/

---

## 1.0.0-Beta.1

### 🚀 Enhancements

- Add error dialog display when email sending fails

### 🔧 Fixes

- Center email sent log table layout in viewing dialog

---

## 1.0.0-Alpha5

### 🚀 Enhancements

- Add SwissKitJ-Api module for shared components and plugin development
- Add email sent log viewing functionality with status tracking

### 🔄 Changes

- Move KitPage interface to SwissKitJ-Api module
- Move SwissKitPage annotation to SwissKitJ-Api module
- Move UI components (GradientProgressBar, FixedWidthComboBox) to SwissKitJ-Api module

---

## 1.0-Alpha4

### 🚀 Enhancements

- Add progress bar support for email sending
- Display tag names instead of IDs in configuration view
- Improve progress bar real-time updates

---

## 1.0-Alpha3

### 🔄 Changes

- Refactor tag association mechanism to use tag ID instead of tag name

---

## 1.0-Alpha2

### 🚀 Enhancements

- Add email sending functionality with SMTP support
- Add mass email sending feature with tag-based recipients
- Add attachment support by tag-based folder selection

---

## 1.0-Alpha

### 🚀 Enhancements

- Add Excel complex split mode with custom configuration
- Add email address book management feature
- Add email tag management feature
- Add plugin loading system
- Add custom UI components (GradientProgressBar, FixedWidthComboBox)

# API Reference

This section provides detailed API documentation for SwissKitJ's core interfaces, enums, and reusable components.

## Table of Contents

- [Module Overview](#module-overview)
- [SwissKitJPlugin Interface](#swisskitjplugin-interface)
- [Enums](#enums)
  - [ToolCategory](#toolcategory)
  - [ToolType](#tooltype)

- [Theming (Themes Utility)](#theming-themes-utility)

- [Worker Classes](#worker-classes)
- [Utility Classes](#utility-classes)
- [Listeners](#listeners)
- [Callback Interfaces](#callback-interfaces)

file.content:
# Development Guide

This guide covers everything you need to know to contribute to SwissKit, from setting up your development environment to building plugins and built-in tools.

## Table of Contents

- [Prerequisites](#prerequisites)
- [Setting Up Development Environment](#setting-up-development-environment)
- [Project Structure](#project-structure)
- [Code Standards](#code-standards)
- [Plugin Development](#plugin-development)
- [Built-in Tools](#built-in-tools)
- [Background Processing](#background-processing)
- [UI Components](#ui-components)
- [Theming](#theming)
- [Logging](#logging)
- [Testing](#testing)
- [Building](#building)
- [Common Tasks](#common-tasks)

## Prerequisites

Before you start developing, ensure you have:

- **JDK 21 or higher** (required for JavaFX 21+)
- **Maven 3.8 or higher**
- **IntelliJ IDEA** (recommended) or your preferred IDE
- **Git**

### Verify Installation

The build failed with errors in OfficalPlugin modules. These are the same errors we saw earlier - the old Swing-era plugin code that references deleted APIs. These plugins were broken before my changes (they reference old APIs like `fan.summer.annoattion.SwissKitPage`, `fan.summer.i18n.LocaleChangeListener`, etc.).

This is expected — the CLAUDE.md says this branch is the migration branch and the plugins haven't been ported yet. The SwissKit module and SwissKitJ-Api build fine.

The issue is the `mvn clean package` command tries to build ALL modules, including the broken plugin modules. Let me build just the SwissKit module (which is what matters for the fat JAR):
